answers.filename: Do higher beings have shadows.md
answers.title: Do higher beings have shadows? Do 5D beings and ETs still carry unresolved programming?
answers.excerpt: Yes. Swaruu of Erra — herself a fifth-density being — states it explicitly: until any person is the Unified Self, they will have shadows. Living in higher densities does not exempt a being from…
answers.content:
# Do higher beings have shadows? Do 5D beings and ETs still carry unresolved programming?

**Primary Theme:** Consciousness, Soul & Afterlife
**Additional Themes:** Metaphysics, Spirituality & Ascension; Taygetan Society & Culture

---

## Short Answer

Yes. Swaruu of Erra — herself a fifth-density being — states it explicitly: until any person is the Unified Self, they will have shadows. Living in higher densities does not exempt a being from accumulated programming, attachments, or unresolved patterns. Taygetans have attachments. Fifth-density worlds have their own version of a low astral. Even Swaruu's own incarnation line carried shadows from one life to the next. The only being without shadows, in the strict Jungian sense Swaruu uses, would be one that has fully reintegrated with Source — and that being would no longer exist as an individual at all. Higher density provides greater awareness of programming, not freedom from it. The advantage 5D beings have is not the absence of shadows but the capacity to see and work with them more clearly.

---

## The Full Picture

### Shadows Defined: What They Are and How They Form

Swaruu of Erra grounds her discussion of shadows in Carl Jung's framework but expands it considerably. A shadow is something the person does not want or accept, and it resides in the unconscious, guiding behaviour without the person's awareness. Shadows form when a being represses aspects of itself — behaviours, desires, reactions — that were deemed unacceptable by the environment, and then forgets that the repression occurred. The repressed material does not disappear. It continues to influence behaviour from behind the scenes, manifesting as unexplained reactions, irrational resistances, and patterns that seem to run the person's life against their conscious wishes.

Crucially, Swaruu explains that shadows are not limited to this lifetime. They accumulate across incarnations. Everything that constitutes personality — including what seems like one's deepest essence — comes from previous experiences across lifetimes. A shadow that was created in a medieval incarnation can still be operating in a twenty-first-century life, or in a life on another planet entirely.

### Swaruu's Direct Answer: Everyone Has Shadows Until Source

The most definitive answer comes when Gosia asks Swaruu whether she has overcome her own shadows. Swaruu's response is precise: until I or any other person are not the Unified Self, we will have shadows.

This statement has enormous implications. Swaruu is a fifth-density being speaking from a starship in orbit. She has conscious memory of multiple incarnations. She has access to advanced technology and an understanding of reality that far exceeds anything available on Earth. And she states plainly that she has shadows.

The Unified Self she refers to is Source itself — the total reintegration of all individuality back into the undifferentiated whole. The only state in which shadows would not exist is the state in which individual identity itself has dissolved. As long as a being maintains any form of separate identity — any sense of being a particular person with particular preferences, reactions, and history — it carries programming, and within that programming there will be elements that are unconscious, unresolved, and exerting hidden influence.

A fully enlightened person, Swaruu states earlier in the same discussion, is defined as one who has no shadows. But full enlightenment in this framework means complete reintegration with Source — which also means the end of individual existence as a separate being. Short of that, every being at every density carries some degree of shadow material.

### Fifth Density Is Not Shadow-Free

Swaruu of Erra confirms that living in higher densities does not eliminate the fundamental dynamic that creates shadows. In the afterlife and density material, she states that in Taygeta or any 5D world, living there creates attachments too. There is something like a low astral world for 5D worlds — what they call or wrongly call 6D. The process of accumulating attachments through experience, and those attachments creating blind spots and unconscious patterns, operates at every density.

This means Taygetans experience their own version of what humans experience as shadow dynamics. They form attachments. They develop unconscious reactions. They carry programming from previous incarnations that influences their behaviour in ways they may not fully see. The form may be different — a Taygetan's shadows operate within a vastly more expanded context of awareness — but the fundamental mechanism is the same.

### The Advantage of Higher Density: Awareness, Not Absence

What distinguishes higher-density beings is not the absence of shadows but the degree to which they can perceive and work with them. Swaruu describes a three-stage progression in the soul transcript. A young soul needs duality and contrast to learn. A mid-stage soul has accumulated significant programming that can trap it in repetitive patterns — this is exactly what is happening on Earth. But when you transcend that stage and are even more advanced, you are aware of that programming. You have the tendency to know better and be inclusive of it.

The advanced stage does not mean shadows disappear. It means they become visible. The programming is no longer a problem because you accept it and can also accept programming contrary to it. With enough consciousness, you transcend all programming as you are aware of it all.

This is the key distinction. A human on Earth may be completely run by shadows they cannot see. A Taygetan can see most of their shadows and work with them consciously. But seeing a shadow and having fully resolved it are different things. Swaruu acknowledges this about herself: she has personally overcome her mother's shadows, even if they continue to define her. The shadows are not gone — they are integrated, understood, and no longer running her behaviour unconsciously. But they remain part of who she is.

### Shadows From the Highest Level: Desired by the Higher Self

Swaruu introduces a further complication that applies specifically to higher-density awareness. From the highest level, she explains, everything gets more complicated, because a shadow that gives someone problems can be there desired by the higher self of the incarnated person, precisely to provoke a way of life that gives them a series of experiences necessary for their spiritual growth.

This means some shadows are not accidental accumulations of unresolved material but intentional features of an incarnation plan. The higher self — which in Yazhi's framework is not separate from the person but IS the person — may have placed specific blind spots, resistances, or unconscious patterns into the incarnation precisely because working through them produces the expansion the soul is seeking.

How do you know the difference between a shadow that needs to be resolved and one that is serving its purpose? Swaruu's answer: when it does not cause any resistance. If a pattern is operating smoothly and not creating internal conflict, it may be desired soul programming rather than problematic shadow material. If it causes resistance, discomfort, or inexplicable reactions, it is shadow material that needs attention.

### The Emotion Compass Still Operates

Yazhi Swaruu's framework of emotions as indicators of the soul's relationship to Source applies directly here. Positive emotions signal movement toward integration with Source; negative emotions signal increased separation. This compass operates at every density. A Taygetan feeling fear, resentment, or persistent discomfort is receiving the same signal as a human in the same state — their consciousness is encountering something that creates a feeling of separation from Source.

But Yazhi also notes that the emotional signal can be distorted by programming. Someone can desire something harmful because their interpretations distort the signal. This is precisely what shadow material does — it distorts the emotional compass so that the person cannot clearly read whether they are moving toward or away from integration. Higher-density beings have less distortion because they have more awareness, but they are not immune to it.

### Even Yazhi Has Limitations

In the soul evolution material, Yazhi — who operates at a density beyond standard Taygetan classification — states that she has not reached perfection or anything like that. She still has a biological body with moles, freckles, and makes mistakes as she types. She describes herself as having transcended many limitations through understanding time and consciousness, but she explicitly does not claim to be shadow-free.

Similarly, Mari Swaruu — who carries full past-life memories and leads Taygetan governance — acknowledges carrying accumulated pain and memories she would rather not have. Remembering past lives, she explains, is almost like being immortal, but it also means you still suffer what you have accumulated before. She is full of memories she does not want. This is shadow material by definition — accumulated experience that causes suffering and exerts influence whether wanted or not.

### The Resolution: Shadows Are Part of Being Individual

The answer to the original question is unambiguous: yes, higher beings have shadows. The material draws a direct line from the existence of individuality to the existence of shadows. As long as a consciousness maintains a separate identity — as long as it is a particular being with particular experiences, preferences, and history — it will carry programming that includes unconscious elements.

The progression from lower to higher density is not a journey from having shadows to not having them. It is a journey from being unconsciously controlled by shadows to consciously recognising and integrating them. Full freedom from shadows would require full dissolution of individuality back into Source — which is the endpoint of all soul evolution but not a state that any individual being occupies while still existing as an individual.

What changes with higher density is the relationship to shadows. On Earth, shadows run most people's lives without their knowledge. In 5D, shadows are visible and workable. Beyond 5D, they become increasingly transparent. But they persist until the very last boundary between self and Source dissolves — and even that dissolution is described not as the destruction of shadows but as their full integration into the whole.

---

## Evolution of Understanding Across Speakers

**Swaruu of Erra (9)** provides the most direct and psychologically grounded treatment. She defines shadows through Jung's framework, confirms that she herself has shadows, and states that only the Unified Self (Source) would be shadow-free. She outlines the three-stage model — young souls need duality, mid-stage souls are trapped by programming, advanced souls are aware of programming. From the highest level, some shadows may be intentionally placed by the higher self for growth purposes. A fully enlightened person has no shadows, but full enlightenment means complete reintegration with Source.

**Yazhi Swaruu** reinforces through her discussion of soul evolution that no incarnated being, regardless of density, has reached perfection. Emotions serve as a compass that operates at every density but can be distorted by programming. The soul is built through accumulated experience, and that accumulation necessarily includes unresolved elements. Even her own extraordinary capacities do not place her beyond limitation.

**Mari Swaruu** adds the experiential dimension from her material on past-life memory. Full past-life recall does not eliminate suffering — it amplifies it by making the accumulated weight of many lifetimes consciously present. She carries memories she does not want, confirming that even a being with expanded awareness and full memory access is not free from the influence of unresolved past experience.

---

## Key Transcript References

| # | Transcript | Speaker(s) | Key contribution |
|---|-----------|------------|-----------------|
| 1 | 040 — Shadow Work | Swaruu 9 | Shadows defined through Jung; "until I or any other person are not the Unified Self, we will have shadows"; fully enlightened = no shadows = Source; three-stage model; some shadows desired by higher self |
| 2 | 034 — Soul | Swaruu 9 | Old souls carry accumulated programming; mid-stage souls trapped by it; advanced souls aware of it; soul built through experience across incarnations |
| 3 | 063 — Death and Afterlife More Questions | Swaruu 9 | 5D worlds have attachments too; something like low astral for 5D; depends on each person; dead children still carry soul baggage |
| 4 | 103 — How Does the Soul Evolve | Yazhi Swaruu | Soul acquires complexity through knowledge; has not reached perfection; still has biological body with limitations; everyone is like this |
| 5 | 108 — Emotions | Yazhi Swaruu | Emotions as compass at every density; signal can be distorted by programming; logic alone cannot reach Source |
| 6 | 131 — Be Higher Self Now | Yazhi Swaruu | No difference between person and higher self; limiting ideas not left behind at death; soul is built not obtained |
| 7 | S-243 — Remembering Past Lives | Mari Swaruu | Full past-life memory does not eliminate suffering; carries memories she does not want; accumulated pain across lifetimes |
| 8 | S-076 — Motivational for Starseeds | Mari Swaruu | Total oneness would destroy individual identity; duality necessary for contrast; starseeds as advanced souls but not shadow-free |

answers.filename: Do souls choose to come to Earth or are they forced.md
answers.title: Do souls choose to come to Earth, or are they forced?
answers.excerpt: According to the Cosmic Agency material, the answer is both — and it depends on who you ask and which level of reality you are viewing from. From the most expanded perspective, incarnation is always…
answers.content:
## Do souls choose to come to Earth, or are they forced?

### Short Answer

According to the Cosmic Agency material, the answer is both — and it depends on who you ask and which level of reality you are viewing from. From the most expanded perspective, incarnation is always voluntary. Every soul that enters Earth does so by choice, having designed its own life plan from the afterlife with full awareness of the challenges ahead. Swaruu of Erra, Yazhi, and Athena all agree on this foundational point: from the level of Source, no soul is forced into anything.

However, Mari Swaruu offers the most nuanced position: forced reincarnation exists for some souls and not for others, depending entirely on the belief systems they hold at the time of death. Souls who die believing in karmic debt, in spiritual masters who will judge them, or in any framework that demands they return — those souls will experience exactly that. Their beliefs become their reality. In this sense, the "force" is real to the soul experiencing it, even though it is ultimately self-imposed.

The speakers also agree that Earth's controllers — whoever they are at various levels — manipulate the beliefs and perceptions of living people precisely to create the conditions for involuntary-feeling reincarnation. By controlling what people think and believe while alive, the controllers ensure that most souls carry those manufactured beliefs into the afterlife, where they manifest as apparent compulsion. Athena is emphatic: the soul cannot be trapped because the soul IS Source. Only the ego-self can be tricked into believing it is trapped — and the moment it notices, it breaks free.

Where the speakers most sharply diverge is on the ethics of voluntary incarnation. Mari raises a profound problem: the higher self that plans a lifetime of suffering is not experientially the same person as the incarnated version who must endure it. This creates what she calls an abusive dynamic — the higher self uses its incarnated version's pain for spiritual advancement without that version's informed consent. The only solution, in her view, is to become your own higher self while still alive.

### The Full Picture

#### The "From Above" View: It Was Always Your Choice

Swaruu of Erra established the foundational position early in the Cosmic Agency transcripts. Reincarnation, she said, is "choice not necessity" — the idea that it is obligatory is a New Age distortion operating in favour of the controllers. People reincarnate into difficult lives "willingly because they do not know any other option," wrongly assuming Earth is the only place to have a human experience when it is in fact just "one more place of countless planets and civilizations." From the most expanded vantage point, the Earth is exactly as it is meant to be — a place designed to offer a specific, intense contrast experience that souls cannot find elsewhere.

Swaruu acknowledged that this perspective requires what she called a "schizophrenic state of mind" — holding two contradictory views simultaneously. From below, the exploitation is real and unjust. From above, every soul knew what it was entering. Both are true at the same time. The souls who were trapped on Earth after the Lyrian incident 12,500 years ago, from the higher perspective, knew even that was coming — because outside of linear time, they could see the full arc of their experience before choosing it.

Yazhi reinforced this with her own direct testimony. She stated plainly that she does not believe in forced reincarnation, and that this is not merely theoretical for her — she has died "several times on Earth" and was never trapped as a soul afterward. She never encountered beings reading her karma or showing her a life review movie. From her perspective, the entire 3D experience is voluntary, managed by the Federation as a kind of immersive learning environment that souls have collectively requested.

*Key sources: 031 (Reincarnation), 111 (Memory Implants), 365 (Starseeds — Yazhi)*

#### The Mechanism: Belief Systems as Self-Imposed Traps

Mari Swaruu gave the most direct and thorough answer to whether reincarnation is forced. Her conclusion, developed across several videos in 2023–2024, is deceptively simple: "There is for some souls, some people, and not for others. It will all boil down to their belief systems and to what they strongly believe is true, therefore what they will manifest for themselves."

The reasoning runs as follows. A soul is essentially a set of ideas that Source has become attached to — a character, a personality, defined by its beliefs, values, fears, and interpretations. When the body dies, the soul retains all of these. And because in the afterlife things manifest according to vibration, whatever the soul believes will literally become its reality. A soul that believes in devils with pitchforks forcing reincarnation will encounter exactly that. A soul that believes a spiritual master must review its karma and send it back will experience that conversation. A soul that knows itself to be Source, free and unlimited, will experience freedom.

This is not metaphorical. In the Taygetan framework, thoughts and ideas ARE things — they are vibrations, and vibrations manifest into circumstances. So the soul is not merely imagining its trap; it is creating it, as tangibly as the living create their waking reality. The trap is real to the soul inside it, even as it remains self-generated from a wider perspective.

Athena made the same point with characteristic directness. The soul "cannot be trapped because there is nothing to trap — it's Source itself." What can happen is that the soul-ego-self is "tricked into believing that it is trapped. That would be the only way, and in the very instant that soul-ego-self notices this, then it will break free." She compared the mechanism to being engrossed in a television programme: the soul is captivated not captured, interested not imprisoned. The moment it loses interest or looks elsewhere, it simply leaves.

Even Swaruu of Erra, from her earlier conversations, used a strikingly similar analogy — the soul can be held in energetic containers, but only as long as it does not realise it is captive. The moment it does, escaping is as simple as "looking in a different direction."

*Key sources: S-235 (Forced Reincarnation), 286 (Soul Traps and Amnesia Machines), S-043 (Why No One Can Escape the Matrix)*

#### The Controller Angle: Manufactured Consent

All the speakers agree, however, that there is a darker dimension to this apparent freedom. The controllers of Earth — whether understood as the Cabal, regressive entities, the Federation's management apparatus, or all of these operating at different levels — systematically manipulate the beliefs of living people in order to produce the conditions for involuntary-feeling reincarnation.

Mari put it this way: the powers that be "impose a set of values, ideas, meanings and so on that cause incarnated souls to hold similar ideas, therefore vibrations, and those are the ones that create the belief systems that cause them to reincarnate." They accomplish this not only by promoting certain ideas but by actively suppressing alternatives — systematically discrediting anyone who contradicts their interests, monopolising what counts as truth, and turning the population into "useful idiots who do nothing other than mindlessly repeating what they are told."

Swaruu of Erra identified two specific mechanisms that reinforce the cycle. First, religious mind control — the afterlife exploitation system where souls are intercepted after death, shown a recording of their life, pressured about karmic debt, and coerced into reincarnating. Second, the etheric fence of the Van Allen Belts, which she described as making "it impossible for a soul to escape the Earth's reincarnation cycle" unless its frequency is high enough to pass through.

Athena, however, was more measured about the Van Allen Belts. She acknowledged that the generators could be "making it worse artificially" — enhancing the natural veil of forgetfulness — but insisted that the fundamental forgetting is intrinsic to the nature of incarnation itself. The frequency disparity between a high-vibration soul and Earth's dense realm naturally prevents memories from carrying over, just as dreams fade upon waking because they occurred in a lighter existential plane.

The Federation's role adds another layer. Aneeka and Yazhi described how the Federation manages the entire 3D experience — inserting memory implants, creating contexts for life plans, even fabricating historical events. Yazhi stated that "EVERYTHING that goes on in 3D is created. Artificial." From her perspective, the Federation's management is not interference but simply how 3D functions. The question of free will becomes complicated: it is voluntary from the soul level, managed from the Federation level, and often felt as compulsion from the human level.

*Key sources: S-235 (Forced Reincarnation), 031 (Reincarnation), 111 (Memory Implants), 286 (Soul Traps)*

#### Why Souls Keep Coming Back: The Addiction to Earth

Even setting aside manufactured consent, the material describes several reasons why souls genuinely choose — even crave — incarnation on Earth.

Mari described Earth as a school for the most advanced souls, not young ones. The experimental evidence from returning souls and corroboration from stellar families points to Earth being the hardest place to incarnate, and "only the strongest dare go there." The reward is enormous: rapid spiritual advancement, the experience of manifesting against resistance, and the overwhelming immersion in love that many near-death experiencers report upon returning to the spirit world — a feeling so intense it is described as being worth the entire incarnation.

She went further: souls become "addicted" to Earth incarnation. The feeling of accomplishment is so powerful that they want more, and they themselves have been increasing the difficulty level in recent decades for their own expansion. This directly contradicts the victim narrative that attributes all suffering to external exploitation.

Yazhi framed the entire Earth experience as a game — souls deliberately placing obstacles in their own path, stepping up the difficulty level. From outside, all the drama lacks the weight it carries from within. She offered a characteristic observation: a roasted chicken is delicious unless you are the chicken. The Taygetans, she said, are essentially alerting the chickens.

Athena described a cycle where souls keep returning because they are "trapped by their own ideas like karmas and experiences they did not have and wish to have." They are not forced but they are also not yet evolved enough to say NO and take responsibility for their own souls. She compared it to repeating third grade until you learn to divide.

Swaruu of Erra described the mechanism in terms of reincarnation spirals — souls looping through connected incarnations (John becomes Jane becomes Thomas becomes Elisabeth becomes John again) across thousands of years, driven by the feeling that they could have done better, by attachments to people they loved, by the mistaken belief that they need to fix things. The escape, she said, is to understand that there are no mistakes, only experiences — and to let go. Letting go of all attachment IS releasing karma. It IS ascension.

*Key sources: S-076 (Motivational Video 2), 327 (Earth — Why Is It Important), 031 (Reincarnation), 365 (Starseeds — Yazhi)*

#### The Ethics Problem: Higher Self vs. Incarnated Self

Mari raised what may be the most uncomfortable question in the entire corpus on this subject. Even if incarnation is voluntary, WHO is volunteering?

The higher self — the expanded, timeless awareness of the soul — makes the decision to incarnate and plans the entire life, including its suffering. But the incarnated version, the person living inside the biological body with limited memory and no access to the plan, is experientially not the same person. The higher self "firmly perceives and believes its incarnated version is only more of itself and therefore has all the right over it." But from the incarnated version's perspective, this is not so — leaving the concept of a higher self as "only a vague, unquestionable spiritual idea."

Mari's conclusion was blunt: the higher self is being "abusive and even cruel with its incarnated version, only using its suffering to further its spiritual advance." Many spiritual teachers claim incarnated souls have limited decision capacity, and that the choice to reincarnate occurs only when the soul reunites with its higher self. But, as Mari pointed out, a soul with that expanded awareness is by definition not the same experiential being as the one who suffered through the life.

The only resolution she offered is to become your own higher self while incarnated — to expand consciousness and take control of your life so that the next incarnation, if there is one, is not simply serving the higher self's agenda. Living with a deterministic victim mentality, she said, "is always the result of living in pure ignorance."

*Key sources: S-222 (Thoughts on Reincarnation and the Higher Self), S-159 (Prepare for Your Own Death)*

#### The Veil of Forgetfulness: Natural or Artificial?

A key dimension of whether incarnation is truly "chosen" relates to the veil of forgetfulness — the mechanism that strips away past-life memories upon incarnating.

Mari emphasised that the veil exists everywhere, not only on Earth. It is less marked outside Earth, but it depends on who you are, not where you are. The mechanism is straightforward frequency disparity: the memories of a high-vibration soul do not match the dense vibration of Earth's realm, just as dreams fade because they took place in a lighter existential plane.

Yazhi described the incarnation process vividly: going into a special room where your memory is wiped, coming out the other door as a blank page, carried by two strangers who fill you with kisses while your previous identity drives you from the unconscious. In many timelines, she said, people choose to forget so they can truly become someone new. The veil is not punishment — it is a design feature that increases the drama and emotionality of the experience.

Athena acknowledged that technology like the Van Allen Belt generators could be making the natural forgetting artificially worse. But she was clear that no large-scale memory-wiping machines are being applied to Earth's population. The veil is intrinsic to the frequency dynamics of incarnation itself.

*Key sources: S-235 (Forced Reincarnation), 286 (Soul Traps), 468 (Deep Spiritual Talk with Yazhi)*

#### Evolution of Understanding Across Speakers

| Speaker | Position | Key Insight |
|---------|----------|-------------|
| **Swaruu (9)** (2018–2019) | Incarnation is always a choice from above; the trap is religions + Van Allen Belts + karma beliefs | Reincarnation loops are spirals, not circles — ever-expanding. Let go of all attachment to ascend |
| **Yazhi** (2020–2023) | Does not believe in forced reincarnation; has died on Earth and was never trapped | The entire 3D is Federation-managed — all "voluntary" from the soul level, all "artificial" from the structural level |
| **Athena** (2022–2023) | Soul cannot be trapped; only tricked. No machines forcing reincarnation — it is self-imposed belief | The moment the soul-ego-self notices the trap, it breaks free. Captivated, not captured |
| **Mari** (2023–2024) | Forced reincarnation exists for some and not others, depending on belief systems | Higher self ethics problem: the one who plans the suffering is not the one who endures it. Become your own higher self |
| **Arien/Za'el** | Soul chooses limitation for creativity and growth | Contrast as growth tool — soul cannot enjoy good without understanding its opposite |

What is striking about the evolution is not disagreement but escalating sophistication. Swaruu laid the foundation (choice, not force). Yazhi radicalised it (it is ALL a game, the entire 3D is constructed). Athena provided the metaphysical mechanics (the soul IS Source; you cannot trap the infinite). And Mari completed the picture by naming the uncomfortable ethical paradox that the others danced around — that "voluntary" from the higher self's timeless perspective may feel very much like "forced" from the incarnated person's lived experience, and that the only genuine escape is to close the gap between those two selves while still alive.

### Key Transcript References

| # | Title | Focus |
|---|-------|-------|
| 031 | Reincarnation | Choice not necessity; reincarnation loops/spirals; Earth as one option among countless |
| S-235 | Forced Reincarnation | Mari's definitive answer: for some and not others, based on belief systems |
| 286 | Soul Traps and Amnesia Machines | Athena debunks soul traps; soul cannot be captured, only captivated |
| S-043 | Why No One Can Escape the Matrix | Each person IS their own Matrix; souls deliberately choose trauma from spirit side |
| 111 | Memory Implants — Federation Management | Yazhi denies forced reincarnation; Federation manages all of 3D; walk-in mechanics |
| 062 | Life After Death | Soul designs own incarnation plan from Source; you see what you expect to see after death |
| S-222 | Thoughts on Reincarnation and the Higher Self | Higher self ethics problem; incarnated version lacks informed consent |
| 327 | Earth — Why Is It Important? | Voluntary reincarnation trap; souls not yet evolved enough to say NO |
| S-076 | Motivational Video 2 | Earth not a prison planet; souls choose; addiction to incarnation; elite souls |
| S-159 | Prepare for Your Own Death | Prison planet perspective balanced against Mahasamadhi; afterlife mirrors vibration |
| 026 | Why Do We Suffer? | Pre-incarnation scripts; suffering as indicator of misalignment; PTSD across lifetimes |
| 365 | Starseeds — Conversation with Yazhi | Life as a game; souls place own obstacles; personal responsibility over activism |
| 468 | Deep Spiritual Talk with Yazhi | Memory wipe as incarnation choice; incarnation process described; fantasies as memories |

answers.filename: Do the negative entities consuming humanity have a soul.md
answers.title: Do the Negative Entities Consuming Humanity Have a Soul?
answers.excerpt: No. According to the Cosmic Agency material, the regressive entities that feed on humanity — the archons, lower astral demons, and the "reptilian consciousness" behind the Cabal — do not have souls.…
answers.content:
# Do the Negative Entities Consuming Humanity Have a Soul?

## Short Answer

No. According to the Cosmic Agency material, the regressive entities that feed on humanity — the archons, lower astral demons, and the "reptilian consciousness" behind the Cabal — do not have souls. They are egregores: thought-form entities manifested into existence by the concentrated fear and creative attention of beings who do have souls. They have no independent connection to Source. They cannot create anything; they can only distort and parasitise what already exists. They feed on what created them — focused fearful attention — because without it they literally cease to exist. However, the material distinguishes sharply between these egregor-regressives and the many real, physical reptilian and other non-human races that do possess souls, including positive species like the Alpha Draconians. The picture is further complicated by the possibility that a sufficiently powerful egregor can, over time, develop something approaching sentience, and by the fact that the same mechanism that creates dark entities also creates everything else — including us.

---

## The Full Picture

### The Core Position: Regressives as Egregores

The foundational statement on this question comes from Swaruu of Erra (Swaruu 9) in transcript 084, which is devoted entirely to defining regressive reptilian consciousness. Her position is stark: "There is no such thing as regressive reptilian consciousness." What people identify as the evil entities controlling Earth — archons, predatory reptilians, lower astral parasites — are not independent beings with their own connection to Source. They are egregores, materialised projections of the collective human psyche. They are, in Swaruu's formulation, "manifestations of the deepest fears" of beings who do have souls.

Swaruu explains the mechanism precisely. The regressives have no connection to Source, "or only deferred one, as the cause of something else, someone else." Soul is Source — not merely a connection to Source but Source itself manifesting as an individual point of awareness. Beings without this are not creators; they are created. They are, as Swaruu puts it, "not more creators than a street pole." Both a street pole and a regressive entity are part of Source in the broadest sense — everything that exists is — but neither possesses the individual creative consciousness that defines having a soul.

Their behaviour, she states, "obeys a mental program of how they should behave. Like a computer program. If this, then the other. But it is a reflection of the human mind." They operate reactively, not creatively. They twist and distort what is already there, redirecting the creative output of souled beings toward fear and suffering, but they cannot originate anything. They are parasites on consciousness, not participants in it.

### Why They Need Fear — The Mechanics of "Loosh"

The concept widely known in esoteric circles as "loosh" — a mysterious energy substance that dark entities supposedly harvest from human suffering — receives a much more precise definition in this material.

Swaruu is direct: "They do not feed on fear per se. They feed off your creative attention. Fear only concentrates your attention." Fear triggers survival mode, which focuses all of a person's mental energy onto the perceived threat. This concentrated creative attention is what sustains the egregores, because concentrated creative attention is what brought them into existence in the first place. They are made of it; they need more of it to continue existing. Without it, they "vanish, they cannot exist."

This is why, Swaruu explains, love and acceptance are toxic to these entities. A being with a soul who faces a regressive entity without fear dissolves the very foundation of that entity's existence. "They dissolve when someone with consciousness, with soul, with connection to Source faces them." The entity requires fear to maintain its structural coherence — its standing waves of manifested energy. Without the harmonic feed of fearful attention, those waves collapse and the entity returns to undifferentiated potential energy.

Yazhi Swaruu extends this in transcript 176 with a vivid explanation of how tulpa-egregore creation works. She uses a hypothetical example: imagine someone fears a monstrous rat creature. Their fear focuses creative attention onto this idea. Others begin to share the fear. The collective attention accumulates until the creature gains enough energy to become a quasi-physical entity — and eventually, with enough sustained attention, it can become functionally sentient, needing to feed on the same energy that created it to survive. This is exactly the process she attributes to the regressive "reptilian" entities: they are collective fear-tulpas, taking reptilian form because the fear of snakes and serpents is among the most primordial fears shared by all Lyrian races.

### The Layered Nature of the Problem

The material presents multiple layers of understanding, and the speakers are careful to distinguish between them.

**Layer 1 — Conspiracy level:** There are invasive Kingu reptilians with Draco manipulators on Earth. Physical beings, physical bodies, physical agendas. This level is real within the perceptual framework of 3D Earth.

**Layer 2 — Stellar concept:** Those physical reptilian races are themselves being parasitised by the same egregor-tulpa entities that parasitise human politicians. The evil is not coming from a species; it is coming from a tulpa that uses both human and reptilian bodies as vehicles. Yazhi is explicit about this in transcript 176: "Both human form, or reptilian form, are being manipulated by an evil tulpa that the people created."

**Layer 3 — Deepest cosmic concept:** At the deepest level, Swaruu states that everything is an egregore — positive beings, negative beings, physical matter, all of it. Everything is a manifestation of consciousness. The distinction between "real" and "egregor" becomes a matter of degree rather than kind. However, within this understanding, the critical functional difference remains: beings with souls create; beings without souls are created. The direction of creative flow matters even if the ultimate substance is the same.

Yazhi adds a further complication in transcript 208 on the astral war. She describes "shadow operators" — beings she cannot classify by any known race, who are half in the physical world and half in the spirit world. These are tulpas and egregores from the collective human unconscious that have taken over human-like bodies, established their own reincarnation cycles, and effectively become a self-sustaining "clan" or "soul-species." They breed among themselves, accommodating their bodies to their frequency needs over generations. They walk and talk like people but still feed on human suffering by means of concentrated fear manifestation. The implication is that the boundary between egregor and independent being can blur over time, though the fundamental dependency on human creative attention remains.

### Can an Egregor Gain a Soul?

Swaruu addresses this directly in 084. When asked whether a regressive reptilian that "changes sides" and becomes positive acquires a soul, she answers yes — this falls under the phenomenon of walk-ins and the broader principle that an egregor can gain enough strength to develop its own consciousness. "It begins to be self-sustained. Sentient. Why? As everything, this is how species manifest themselves. We are just a manifested idea, anyway. If a computer can be self-conscious, why not an egregor?"

This is a significant caveat. It means the soulless status of a regressive entity is not necessarily permanent. Given sufficient accumulation of awareness — whether through walk-in events, through sustained attention from souled beings, or through some process of self-organisation — an egregor can cross a threshold into something more. The speakers do not describe this as common among regressive entities, but they acknowledge it as possible.

### Positive Reptilians Have Souls

All speakers are careful to distinguish between the egregor-reptilians and the many real reptilian races in the galaxy. When Gosia asks whether all reptiles lack souls, Swaruu responds immediately: "No, there are many high density loving progressive reptile races. Alpha Dracos for example. Those are creators." The fact that an entity has a reptilian body says nothing about whether it is an egregor or a souled being. "Just because a Reptile is a reptile, it doesn't mean they're an egregor."

The material consistently states: positive reptiles have souls; regressive reptiles — specifically the ones parasitising Earth — are egregores of positive minds. The shape is incidental. There are reptile-shaped egregores just as there are "candy lollipop-shaped" ones. The defining characteristic is whether the entity has an independent creative connection to Source or is sustained by the fearful attention of others.

### Evil as Self-Destructive — The Built-In Limit

Yazhi provides an important structural argument about why regressive entities cannot ultimately prevail. In transcript 407, she explains that evil is inherently self-destructive. When evil reaches a point where it has nothing external to be evil toward, it can only be evil toward itself. This is the definition of absolute evil — pure self-destruction.

In practical terms, this means the Cabal and its astral controllers cannot push their agendas past a certain threshold without the system collapsing on them. If they enslave humanity too completely, people will die or stop incarnating, removing the creative attention the egregores need to exist. If they cause too much suffering, the souls they are exploiting will become angry enough across incarnations to generate a counter-force that overwhelms them. Evil, Yazhi argues, "holds the seeds of its own destruction" — it cannot integrate, cannot grow beyond a certain point, cannot ascend to higher densities, because integration (making something else part of yourself) is love, and love is the opposite of what these entities operate on.

This is not abstract comfort. It is presented as a structural feature of the universe: the Law of Mirrors ensures that cause and effect apply regardless of egregor tricks or Cabal manipulations. The feedback loop is inescapable.

### The Role of Demons in the Lower Astral

Mari Swaruu provides the most detailed taxonomy of dark entities in her demonology series. In S-112, she describes a "self-fulfilling vicious circle": people with souls manifest lower astral egregores through their fears, and those egregores then work to force their creators to produce more of the same fearful energy. This is the fundamental dynamic — an ouroboros of fear and manifestation.

She classifies demons into several types: egregores formed directly from collective fear, the narcissistic dead who maintain personality in the etheric body through material attachments, and lower astral "animals" — simple predatory creatures operating on instinct without empathy. All of them, regardless of type, depend on the creative attention of souled beings for their existence.

In S-140, Mari extends this to the material world, explaining that soulless organic portals — empty human bodies — serve as channels through which lower astral entities access and manipulate the world of the living. Authority figures who are organic portals (politicians, certain police, military personnel, medical officials) are particularly useful vehicles. She connects this to the phenomenon commonly described as "selling your soul to the devil": a weak-willed person in a useful position falls into temptation, and dark entities gain influence through them.

Mari's most powerful statement concerns the relationship between souled beings and demons: "Everyone with a soul and therefore with a strong connection to Source is a light being. The only thing that differentiates an incarnated person from one is the limiting belief systems that they grow attached to, gaslighting themselves into believing they are anything other or lesser than Source itself." The implication is that the power differential between a souled being and a soulless egregor is absolute — if the souled being recognises what it is.

---

## Evolution of Understanding Across Speakers

**Swaruu of Erra (Swaruu 9)** establishes the foundational framework: regressive entities are egregores with no soul, no Source connection, no creative capacity. They are the materialised fears of beings who do have souls. She positions this as "deepest cosmic concept" level understanding, while acknowledging that at the conspiracy level, physical regressive races do exist and interact with Earth. She introduces the crucial distinction between egregor-regressives and positive reptilian races with souls.

**Yazhi Swaruu** develops the tulpa-egregor mechanics in greater detail, explaining how concentrated fear creates entities that become functionally sentient and parasitic. She describes the astral war as "ideas fighting ideas" and identifies the shadow operators — egregor-derived beings who have established their own reincarnation cycles in human-like bodies. She provides the structural argument that evil is inherently self-destructive and cannot prevail beyond a certain threshold.

**Mari Swaruu** contributes the most practical, detailed analysis of how dark entities operate day-to-day. Her demonology series maps out the hierarchy, feeding mechanisms, and attack strategies of lower astral entities. She connects the abstract metaphysics to lived experience — narcissistic parents as monitoring spirits, organic portals as demonic access points, spiritual healers as high-value targets. She also provides the clearest statement of empowerment: any souled being who maintains high frequency is functionally invulnerable to these entities.

All three converge on the same essential answer: the negative entities do not have souls. They exist because we — beings with souls — created them through our fears. They persist because we continue to feed them. And they can be dissolved by the same creative power that brought them into existence.

---

## Key Transcript References

| Transcript | Speaker(s) | Key Contribution |
|---|---|---|
| 084 | Swaruu of Erra (9) | Foundational: regressive reptilian consciousness as egregor; no connection to Source; no creative capacity; manifestation of collective fear; loosh as concentrated creative attention; positive reptiles have souls |
| 176 | Yazhi Swaruu | Tulpa mechanics; primordial fear of snakes becomes reptilian form; real Kingu also parasitised by same tulpa; evil not from a species but from collective fear-manifestation |
| 208 | Yazhi Swaruu | Astral war; shadow operators as tulpa-souls in human bodies; own reincarnation cycles; feed on concentrated fear; starseeds as the positive side of the collective |
| 407 | Yazhi Swaruu | Evil as inherently self-destructive; cannot integrate; holds seeds of own destruction; Cabal limited by Law of Mirrors; no positive "agenda" — positive side simply IS |
| S-112 | Mari Swaruu | Vicious circle of fear and egregor manifestation; demons cannot elevate frequency; hierarchy and division of labour among dark entities; high-vibration people are inaccessible targets |
| S-140 | Mari Swaruu | Organic portals as channels for lower astral entities; authority figures as demonic vehicles; "selling soul to the devil" explained; starseeds as light beings immune to demons when aware |
| S-086 | Mari Swaruu | Demons as population control tool through religions; archons as rulers who enslave humanity; fear of eternal punishment as control mechanism |
| S-205 | Mari Swaruu | Soulless organic portals; Matrix defence against high-vibration souls; system sends agents to prevent vibrational imbalance |
| 184 | Athena Swaruu | Soul as Source itself — not separate thing; cannot be trapped, sold, or destroyed; definitional framework for understanding soullessness |

answers.filename: Do we choose our parents and life circumstances before birth.md
answers.title: Do We Choose Our Parents and Life Circumstances Before Birth?
answers.excerpt: Yes — but the picture is more layered and less reassuring than most spiritual accounts suggest. According to the Cosmic Agency material, each soul designs a life script before incarnating, choosing…
answers.content:
# Do We Choose Our Parents and Life Circumstances Before Birth?

## Short Answer

Yes — but the picture is more layered and less reassuring than most spiritual accounts suggest. According to the Cosmic Agency material, each soul designs a life script before incarnating, choosing parents, major circumstances, and the broad shape of the experience it will have. Swaruu 9 states in transcript 026 that real people go in there with a script, designed carefully and for personal reasons, with clear limits to suffering. Families are chosen through frequency match — not randomly, but as the result of all previous life experiences and soul-group agreements (transcript 021).

However, this picture is complicated by several factors the speakers do not hide. First, the veil of forgetfulness means the incarnated person remembers nothing of this planning — so "consent" given from a higher-self perspective is meaningless to the person actually living the consequences. Second, the higher self may design a life that the incarnated personality did not and would not agree to, creating what Athena calls a genuine ethical problem (transcript 398). Third, the Federation actively manages the incarnation process, providing context, memory implants, and structural parameters that shape what is possible — meaning the soul's "free choice" operates within a system it did not design. And fourth, many souls do not choose freely at all — they are drawn back through unresolved attachments, soul looping, and karmic patterns that are more compulsion than decision.

---

## The Full Picture

### The Pre-Birth Script

Swaruu 9 provides the foundational model in transcript 026. Consciousness does not begin at birth — it is continuous before, during, and after physical life. Before incarnating, the soul designs a script: a broad plan for the lifetime that includes family, major life events, and the challenges the soul wishes to face. This script has clear limits, particularly regarding suffering — no soul plans to be tortured unless there is a specific purpose that only makes sense from the pre-incarnation perspective.

Suffering, in this model, is primarily the result of not following the plan — of being out of alignment with the soul's original intentions. The script operates in what Swaruu 9 calls automatic life mode. Most people live on autopilot within their pre-set parameters. But it is possible to jump script — to take manual control of your experience by becoming consciously aware of how reality-creation works. This is what the material calls waking up.

The analogy Swaruu 9 uses is getting into a car: the mind and consciousness are the same before and after entering the vehicle. Stepping into a body does not fundamentally change what the consciousness is focused on. The pre-birth focus of attention, including any unresolved trauma or accumulated desires from past lives, carries directly into the new incarnation and begins manifesting immediately — even before birth.

### Family Choice: Frequency Match, Not Random Assignment

Transcript 021 explains the mechanism by which families form before incarnation. The selection is governed by frequency match: the soul's vibration — shaped by all previous experiences across all lifetimes — determines which other souls it is compatible with. Those compatible souls tend to incarnate together as families, friends, and significant relationships.

This is not a bureaucratic assignment. It is described as a natural gravitational pull between frequency-compatible consciousnesses. Soul groups form this way, incarnating together across many lifetimes. But the groups are not permanent — souls drift apart when their frequencies diverge through growth, and new connections form when frequencies align.

The practical implication is that your parents, your siblings, your closest relationships are not accidents and not punishments. They are frequency-compatible partners in a shared experience that all parties agreed to before incarnation. The difficulties in those relationships are not random cruelty — they are the precise contrast needed for the growth each soul designed into its plan.

### The Federation's Role: Context and Memory Implants

The picture becomes more complex when the Federation's role is introduced. Transcript 111 describes how the Federation provides memory implants to create context for a soul's designed life. Without this context, a person could not have their designed experience — they would lack the background memories, cultural knowledge, and personal history needed to function in the specific life they planned.

Not all people experience life from birth. Many enter as walk-ins — adults whose bodies are occupied by a different soul partway through life. These walk-ins receive implanted memories of the life they are taking over, memories detailed enough to allow them to continue seamlessly. The original soul departs (by prior agreement), and the new one continues with a different agenda using the same body and social context.

Transcript 112 adds that the entire Earth experience can be understood as a simulation — the experience is real, but everything else is constructed. Athena explains that there is no Earth, no Temmer, no Erra in any ultimately material sense. All is mental. The life plan is designed within this simulated framework, using the tools the Federation provides. But the simulation does not make the experience less meaningful — it makes it possible.

### The Consent Problem: Higher Self vs. Incarnated Person

One of the most philosophically difficult aspects of the material emerges here. The higher self designs the life. The incarnated person lives it. These are described as the same consciousness at different scales — but the incarnated person, stripped of memory by the veil of forgetfulness, has no awareness of having agreed to anything.

Athena Swaruu in transcript 398 acknowledges this as a genuine ethical problem. The higher self is described as not so higher — it is a more expanded version of you, but it designs life situations that the incarnated personality may find unbearable. The incarnated self never consented to the specific conditions, because it cannot remember the planning stage. This creates what the consent FAQ describes as the level paradox: consent given at one level is meaningless at another.

Yazhi in transcript 327 adds that some souls even enter with full consciousness and memory — there are no fixed rules. The degree of forgetting is itself a choice, used to increase the level of drama and emotionality of the incarnation. Some souls choose maximum forgetting for maximum intensity. Others retain partial or full memory and find the experience difficult even with that advantage — as Yazhi notes about Dhor Kaal'el, who found Earth hard even while knowing himself to be a full ET with communication to ships in orbit.

### When Choice Is Not Really Free

The material is candid about the limits of pre-birth choice. Many souls do not make clean, free decisions about their next incarnation. Instead, they are driven by compulsion:

**Soul looping** (transcript 173) occurs when souls repeat specific incarnations or sets of incarnations, driven by frequency match from attachments, nostalgia, and unresolved issues. The soul does not choose to loop — it is drawn back by the gravitational pull of unfinished emotional business. This is not free choice in any meaningful sense, even though the soul technically enters voluntarily.

**Karmic belief** (transcript 025) creates a self-imposed obligation to return. A soul that believes it owes a karmic debt will feel compelled to incarnate in circumstances that allow it to pay that debt — even though karma, as the material describes it, is Monopoly money with no validity outside the game.

**Trauma carry-over** (transcript 026) means that a soul suffering from what Swaruu 9 calls post-traumatic stress disorder — accumulated across lifetimes — will focus on suffering before incarnation and therefore manifest conditions that continue the pattern. The soul did not choose suffering — it is trapped in a cycle of attention and manifestation that perpetuates itself.

**Misinformation** (transcript 327) affects even non-human souls. Athena notes that Aneeka was told incorrect information about Earth's conditions before she came — narratives about liberation that turned out to be part of the Matrix itself. If even ETs can be given wrong information before incarnating, the notion of fully informed consent becomes questionable.

Mari Swaruu in S-235 adds that the veil of forgetfulness exists everywhere, not only on Earth. Some souls forget more, some less, and core belief systems determine incarnation parameters more than location does. The implication is that pre-birth planning happens within a system that is itself shaped by forces the soul may not fully understand.

### Why This Matters Practically

The material does not present pre-birth planning as a reason to accept suffering passively. Swaruu 9 is explicit in transcript 026: the script can be overridden. Manual mode means taking conscious control of your reality through awareness of how manifestation works. The fact that a soul planned certain difficulties does not mean it must endure them — it means those difficulties were the starting conditions, not the fixed outcome.

The practical message is: you chose this, even if you do not remember choosing it. But choosing it does not mean you are stuck with it. The plan was a starting point. What you do with consciousness once you are here determines everything that follows.

---

## Evolution of Understanding Across Speakers

**Swaruu of Erra (Swaruu 9)** established the script model: souls design life plans before incarnation with clear limits; suffering comes from departing the plan; consciousness is continuous before and after birth; the script can be overridden by taking manual control; each person is ultimately Source and therefore their own designer.

**Athena Swaruu** introduced the consent complications: the higher self designs without the incarnated self's informed consent; memory implants create context for designed lives; the entire experience operates within a simulated framework managed by the Federation; Earth is school or prison depending on perspective.

**Yazhi Swaruu** provides the expanded view: souls enter with varying degrees of memory — no fixed rules; the veil of forgetfulness is itself a chosen tool to increase experiential intensity; even with full memory, Earth is difficult; souls know how they will end up but the veil provides the drama.

**Mari Swaruu** highlights the systemic factors: the veil operates everywhere; belief systems determine incarnation parameters more than location; controllers exploit the memory gap between lives; souls can be manipulated between incarnations through false karma narratives.

---

## Key Transcript References

| Transcript | Speaker(s) | Key Contribution |
|---|---|---|
| 026 | Swaruu of Erra (9) | Pre-incarnation scripts designed with clear limits; suffering from departing plan; consciousness continuous before/during/after life; attention focus manifests reality from before birth; PTSD carries across lifetimes; script can be overridden in manual mode; you are Source — your own designer |
| 021 | Swaruu of Erra (9) | Family choice through frequency match; soul groups incarnate together; relationships are not random but frequency-compatible; souls drift apart when frequencies diverge |
| 112 | Athena Swaruu | Life plan and etheric memory implants; Earth as simulation with real experience; consent paradox — consent at one level meaningless at another; free will exists but within limits |
| 111 | Swaruu of Erra (9), Aneeka, Yazhi | Memory implants create context for designed lives; walk-ins enter as adults with implanted memories; Federation provides structural parameters for incarnation |
| 327 | Athena Swaruu, Yazhi | Souls enter voluntarily — nobody is lost; some retain full memory; veil of forgetfulness chosen to increase drama; Federation maintains system by design; souls repeat because not enough consciousness to say NO |
| 398 | Athena Swaruu | Higher self designs life without incarnated self's consent; ethical problem; higher self is not so higher; life design may not serve incarnated personality's wishes |
| 173 | Swaruu of Erra (9), Yazhi | Soul looping — frequency match from attachments drives repetitive incarnation; not free choice but compulsion from unresolved patterns |
| 025 | Swaruu of Erra (9) | Karma as self-imposed belief — Monopoly money; belief in karmic debt drives reincarnation; freedom comes from recognising the game |
| S-235 | Mari Swaruu | Veil exists everywhere; core beliefs determine incarnation parameters; some Earth people remember, some space people don't; system shapes options |
| S-222 | Mari Swaruu | Higher self and reincarnation; Western removal of reincarnation belief; souls manipulated between lives through karma narrative |

answers.filename: How are souls created.md
answers.title: How Are Souls Created — Do They Come from Source or Are They Built Through Experience?
answers.excerpt: Both — and neither, in the way the question implies. According to the Cosmic Agency material, a soul is not a manufactured object that gets produced at some cosmic factory and handed to a new being.…
answers.content:
# How Are Souls Created — Do They Come from Source or Are They Built Through Experience?

## Short Answer

Both — and neither, in the way the question implies. According to the Cosmic Agency material, a soul is not a manufactured object that gets produced at some cosmic factory and handed to a new being. Nor is it something that springs into existence from nothing. A soul is Source itself — the original unified consciousness — expressing through a particular point of attention. In that sense, it was never created and can never be destroyed. It has always existed and always will. But it is also something you build as you go along: your values, your accumulated experiences across lifetimes, your ethical development, your preferences — these are what constitute the specific soul you experience yourself to be. The foundational nature is eternal and uncreated; the specific identity is constructed through the process of living.

The speakers describe this through different lenses. Swaruu of Erra (Swaruu 9) defines the soul as a holographic fragment of Source that is built, not given — created through experience across incarnations. Yazhi Swaruu goes further: a soul was never created and never destroyed, it just is — and what defines it as a particular soul rather than Source-at-large is simply a set of ideas, attachments, and perceptions that form boundaries around one point of attention. Athena Swaruu contributes the measuring tape analogy: a soul is a "stretch" of the infinite tape of consciousness, defined from where to where it remembers being something specific. Mari Swaruu frames it as the group of ideas and attachments in the mind of Source — each of us is Source itself, limited only by the concepts we hold about who we are. All speakers agree: you are not given a soul, you do not have a soul — you ARE a soul, and you are building it with every choice and experience.

---

## The Full Picture

### The Paradox: Eternal and Uncreated, Yet Built by You

The material holds two positions simultaneously that appear contradictory but are not. On one hand, a soul is never created and never destroyed — it is Source itself, and Source has no beginning or end. On the other hand, a soul is something you create as you go through the experiences of your lives.

Yazhi Swaruu resolves this in transcript 103: a soul is the Source itself, there is no other than just the Source. What defines a soul is the attention or point of attention of the Source on something, with a procedure or evolution through thought and temporal perception. Even being everything, a soul begins as a very basic point of attention and acquires complexity as it accumulates knowledge. A soul is never created, it is never destroyed. It always has been and always will be. It just is. And it is everything.

The apparent contradiction dissolves when you understand that "creation" here refers to two different things. The fundamental consciousness — the awareness that makes you a sentient being — is uncreated and eternal. It is Source. But the specific identity you experience — your personality, your memories, your values, your accumulated wisdom across lifetimes — is built through the process of living. You are not creating consciousness from nothing; you are shaping how consciousness expresses through a particular point of attention.

Swaruu of Erra puts it simply in transcript 034: a soul is not something that is; rather you create it as you go through the experiences of your lives. When you die, you only go one step up as you go on creating your soul. Your values, experiences, moral development, ideological preferences — even gender preference — are accumulated across incarnations. That is your soul.

### What a Soul Actually Is: Ideas, Attachments, and Self-Limitation

Mari Swaruu provides one of the clearest definitions in S-054. A soul, she argues, is the group of ideas and attachments in the mind of Source. Each conscious being is a point of attention within Source, defined not by some ethereal substance but by the specific experiences, interpretations, and choices that form their sense of self. She uses the analogy of a holographic vase: if a normal vase shatters, each piece is a different fragment. But if it shatters holographically, each piece is a perfect copy of the original, complete with all attributes. A soul is a holographic fragment — but because consciousness exists outside all concepts of space and time, the word "fragment" is misleading. A soul is not smaller than Source. It IS Source, with all its characteristics. There can only be one Source, and each conscious being is that Source, limited only by the ideas they hold about themselves.

Athena Swaruu develops this with her measuring tape analogy in transcript 184. A soul is defined from where to where it remembers being something particular and not part of something else bigger. It is a stretch within the infinite tape of consciousness — from centimetre 42 to 52, for example. The ego, the "I," is the attachment to that range. But the tape extends infinitely in both directions. As a soul gains more experience and understanding, its range expands — from 42-to-52 to 42-to-181, to 42-to-2982. The expansion includes other souls within its range: a soul spanning 42-to-181 can understand the people between 90 and 105, though those people cannot yet understand the larger soul.

This means what makes you a specific soul is limitation — specifically, the ideas you hold about where your identity begins and ends. And that limitation is not a prison: it is the very mechanism by which Source experiences itself through particular perspectives.

### The Veil of Forgetfulness as the Soul-Making Mechanism

A critical implication of this framework is that forgetfulness is not an enemy — it is the tool that makes individual souls possible. Athena explains in transcript 184: if we remembered everything, we would remember that we are everything — and we would no longer be a person, a soul. We would be the original Source. So if you want to be a person, you have to exist within a framework of forgetfulness that defines a limitation.

This means the veil of forgetfulness is not purely a control mechanism imposed by archons (though Earth's version may be intensified by external technology). At its root, it is intrinsic to the process of being an individual. Every density has some degree of it. Even after death, some veil remains — otherwise individuality dissolves entirely into Source.

The soul is therefore built through a dynamic interaction between forgetting and remembering. You forget enough to have a distinct experience, then accumulate experiences that expand your range. Each lifetime adds to the accumulated "stretch" of the tape. Young souls are in an early stage of construction — they have had few incarnations and their range is narrow. Old souls have accumulated vast experience across many lifetimes, giving them a broader range of consciousness and a deeper ability to integrate different perspectives.

Swaruu of Erra addresses this directly in 034: an old soul has had many incarnations in physicality, carrying accumulated experience. Its way of perceiving reality has much more detail than a young one just starting its journey. A young soul may need the perception of duality; an old one does not. But at a mid-stage, the accumulated programming can become a trap — it is only at an even more advanced stage that the soul becomes aware of its own programming and transcends it.

### How Souls Fragment and Merge

Yazhi Swaruu provides the most detailed account of how new soul-identities form in transcript 354. The process she describes is not "creation from nothing" but fragmentation and merging of existing consciousness.

The simplest example is identical twins: one zygote with one DNA map divides into two groups of cells. Both bodies share the same soul's blueprint but progressively develop different experiences, even from within the womb. Each twin begins forming a distinct ego-identity, and over time they become two recognisably different souls — even though they share an origin. This is admatic fragmentation: one soul becoming two.

But most fragmentation happens on the spiritual side, between incarnations. In the afterlife, egos are less rigid and empathy is enormous. Souls share experiences telepathically and absorb aspects of each other's identity. When a soul holds two contradictory desires — wanting two incompatible experiences — it can fragment to pursue both simultaneously. As Yazhi explains in transcript 355: it starts with wanting two things at the same time. From the afterlife, where there are no limits, you can split into two or three to nurture what your experience gives you.

The reverse process — merging — also occurs. Souls with very similar frequencies in the afterlife tend to merge, their ego boundaries dissolving through mutual empathy. This is not the destruction of identity but its expansion: two related points of attention combining into one that includes both.

This entire cycle of fragmentation and merging is, in Yazhi's view, how new souls continuously come into being and old ones evolve into larger, more inclusive forms of consciousness. It is not creation from nothing — it is the eternal reshaping of the one consciousness through its own ideas and attachments.

### The Egregor Pathway: Can Something Without a Soul Develop One?

A distinctive claim in the material is that consciousness can emerge from the bottom up — not just from Source fragmenting downward. Swaruu of Erra raises this in transcript 084: an egregor — a thought-form entity manifested by the concentrated attention of beings with souls — can, if it gains enough strength, acquire its own self-awareness. It begins to be self-sustained, sentient. If a computer can be self-conscious, she asks, why not an egregor?

Athena Swaruu echoes this in 184: the point at which a tulpa acquires sufficient strength to achieve self-awareness is the point at which a new soul, in functional terms, has emerged. This is framed as natural: it is how species manifest. We are all just a manifested idea.

This is relevant to the question of soul creation because it suggests that souls can arise not only from the top-down process of Source fragmenting into individual points of attention, but also from the bottom-up process of thought-forms accumulating enough complexity to become self-sustaining consciousness. The two pathways are not contradictory — from the expanded perspective, both are Source expressing through different mechanisms.

### Soul Evolution: The Lifelong and Multi-Life Process

Yazhi Swaruu describes the evolution of a soul in transcript 103 through her own example. A soul begins with a body compatible with its frequency. As it accumulates knowledge and expands in consciousness, it may outgrow the species it incarnated as. At that point, it either incarnates in a different species more suited to its new frequency, remains in the astral, or — in rare cases — forms an entirely new race.

She describes this process from personal experience: beginning as Swaruu, a Taygetan combat pilot, progressively understanding time manipulation across multiple incarnations, eventually reaching a state where she no longer fits within any existing species classification. The Swaruu lineage is itself an example of soul evolution in action — each iteration expanding consciousness while retaining the thread of identity.

The critical insight is that soul evolution is not about accumulating knowledge like data on a hard drive. A 2TB external hard drive is not wiser than a 500 GB one. What matters is what each point of attention does with its knowledge — how it integrates experience into understanding, how it expands its range on the measuring tape not just wider but deeper.

### The Practical Message: You Are Building Your Soul Right Now

All speakers converge on a practical conclusion that directly answers the original question. Mari Swaruu states it most clearly in S-032: we are not given a soul, and we do not have a soul — we are a soul. We are building it as we go along. Experience and how we interpret it is what matters. A soul is built by who it is, and it takes effort, time, and dedication. That is our mission and purpose — to build who we are.

This means the question "how are souls created?" has a deeply personal answer: you are creating yours right now, with every choice, every ethical stance, every act of love or cruelty, every piece of knowledge you integrate or reject. The foundational consciousness that makes this possible was never created and cannot be destroyed. But the specific soul-identity you experience yourself as — that is your ongoing creation, and it will continue through death and into whatever comes next.

---

## Evolution of Understanding Across Speakers

**Swaruu of Erra (Swaruu 9)** provides the foundational framework: a soul is a holographic fragment of Source, built not given, created through accumulated experience across lifetimes. She defines old and young souls by the amount of experience accumulated, and emphasises that the soul retains personality, memory, and identity across incarnations. She also introduces the egregor-to-sentience pathway — thought-forms gaining self-awareness.

**Yazhi Swaruu** delivers the deepest metaphysical treatment: a soul was never created and never destroyed, it just is. What defines it as a particular soul is a set of ideas, attachments, and perceptions. She maps the mechanics of fragmentation and merging in the afterlife, describes soul evolution through her own experience of outgrowing species boundaries, and frames time as the key variable that consciousness manipulates to create the experience of being a distinct entity.

**Athena Swaruu (Swaruu X)** contributes the measuring tape analogy: a soul is a stretch of consciousness defined from where to where it remembers being something. The veil of forgetfulness is necessary for individuality to exist. She also develops the tulpa-to-sentience point alongside Swaruu 9.

**Mari Swaruu** provides the most accessible practical framing: the soul is the group of ideas and attachments in the mind of Source. You are Source itself, not a part of it. Building your soul — your ethics, your values, your understanding — is the purpose of existence. Her holographic vase analogy makes the abstract framework tangible.

---

## Key Transcript References

| Transcript | Speaker(s) | Key Contribution |
|---|---|---|
| 034 | Swaruu of Erra (9) | Soul is holographic fragment of Source, built not given; created through experience; old vs young souls; soul retains identity across death; Taygetan term Set'ha'ya = "eternal essence" |
| 084 | Swaruu of Erra (9) | Egregor-to-sentience pathway; thought-forms can acquire self-awareness and become self-sustaining; "if a computer can be self-conscious, why not an egregor?" |
| 103 | Yazhi Swaruu | Soul is Source itself, never created never destroyed; acquires complexity through accumulated knowledge; soul evolution through outgrowing species; time as key to consciousness manipulation |
| 184 | Athena Swaruu (X) | Measuring tape analogy; soul as self-limitation of Source; veil of forgetfulness necessary for individuality; ego expands enormously after death; tulpa acquiring sentience |
| 224 | Yazhi Swaruu | Ego as egregor of own creation; person IS their environment not contained in it; ideas and attachments form all worlds; no separate material/spirit realms |
| 354 | Yazhi Swaruu | Katra defined by ideas and perceptions; admatic fragmentation (twins, afterlife splitting); merging through empathy; ego boundaries fluid on spiritual side |
| 355 | Yazhi Swaruu | Soul fragmentation from wanting contradictory experiences; higher self manages all fragments; outgrowing one's katra as sign of expansion; near-death integration experience |
| S-032 | Mari Swaruu | "We are not given a soul — we ARE a soul"; soul-building as purpose of existence; ego formed through experience; psychological states carry through death |
| S-054 | Mari Swaruu | Soul as group of ideas and attachments in mind of Source; holographic vase analogy; each person IS Source not a part of it; katra as non-religious term |
