answers.excerpt: The tunnel of light is not a universal phenomenon and it is not a trap. According to the Cosmic Agency material, what a person sees at death is a direct manifestation of their own beliefs,…
answers.content:
# Why Does the Tunnel of Light Appear After Death — Is It a Trap?

## Short Answer

The tunnel of light is not a universal phenomenon and it is not a trap. According to the Cosmic Agency material, what a person sees at death is a direct manifestation of their own beliefs, expectations, and frequency. Swaruu 9 states in transcript 062 that when you die you see what you wish to see, because in higher planes everything manifests immediately. There can be no archon traps waiting to force souls to reincarnate — that is only found because that is what they expect to see.

The tunnel of light specifically is a Western cultural phenomenon. Swaruu 9 notes in transcript 063 that near-death experience researchers have found the classical tunnel of light is seen nearly exclusively in the West. In Japanese culture, the near-death experiencer finds a river and must cross it. In African cultures, the person waits for relatives to come for them. The visitation of deceased relatives is the only element that appears consistently across all cultures — and even that manifests differently depending on cultural expectations.

Yazhi Swaruu provides the most direct reframing of the light experience in transcript 223: what many people with near-death experiences describe as being surrounded by pure love is actually the dissolution of the ego back into the unified field. From the perspective of someone alive, this dissolution looks like destruction — and that is terrifying. But from the other side, it is integration, unconditional acceptance, and a love so complete that those who experience it regret returning to their bodies. The light is not bait in a trap. It is the natural experience of a consciousness expanding beyond the limitations of the body.

---

## The Full Picture

### What Near-Death Experiencers Actually Report

The Cosmic Agency material draws on the same body of near-death experience research available on Earth — particularly the work of Dolores Cannon — but interprets it through a fundamentally different framework. Rather than treating NDE reports as descriptions of an objective afterlife landscape that everyone will encounter, the speakers treat them as evidence that each person's afterlife is a unique manifestation of their own consciousness.

Swaruu 9 provides the clearest statement of this principle in transcript 062: when a person dies, they will see and experience exactly what they expect to see. If someone believes in Jesus, Jesus they will find. If they believe in Allah, Allah they will find. If they believe in Cosmic Cat, the cat they will find. This is not a defect in the system or evidence of manipulation. It is how consciousness works at every level — you perceive what your frequency allows you to perceive, and in the higher planes where manifestation is immediate, your beliefs become your experienced reality without delay.

Transcript 063 adds empirical weight to this claim by noting that leading NDE researchers have documented dramatic cultural variation in death experiences. The tunnel of light — far from being a universal feature of dying — is a culturally specific phenomenon. The Japanese experience a river. African cultures experience a waiting period for ancestral escorts. What remains constant is only the broad principle: deceased relatives appear, and the experience reflects the dying person's cultural framework. Even the relatives who appear are shaped by expectation — they comfort, they guide, and they promise to make the transition easier, but the form this takes is culturally determined.

Mari Swaruu addresses NDE reports from a different angle in S-253. She notes that many near-death experiencers say the other side is as physical as this side of the living. Rather than taking this as evidence of an objective spirit world with physical properties, Mari interprets it as a reflection of the experiencer's attachment to material existence. People who are deeply identified with their bodies will manifest a material-seeming afterlife because that is what their consciousness knows how to create. The NDE report is not a map of the afterlife — it is a mirror of the experiencer's state of consciousness.

### The Light as Integration, Not Lure

Yazhi Swaruu provides what may be the most important reframing of the light phenomenon in transcript 223. She describes what happens at death as a dissolution of the ego back into the unified etheric field. The experience of being enveloped in overwhelming love and light — the signature feature of positive NDEs — is this dissolution experienced from the inside.

From the perspective of someone alive, the dissolution of the self sounds like annihilation. The ego, built through a lifetime of attachments to identity, relationships, and the body itself, interprets its own dissolution as destruction. This is why death is feared. But from the other side, Yazhi says, what you interpret here as destruction, there it is integration, and a great love that invades you. You swim in pure love, in unconditional acceptance.

The light, in this framework, is not something external that appears to the dying person. It is the person's own nature — their connection to Source, which was always present but filtered out by the body's narrow perceptual bandwidth. When the body fails and that filter is removed, the consciousness expands and begins to perceive its own fundamental nature, which is Source itself. The experience of light and love is the experience of remembering what you actually are.

This is why those who return from near-death experiences overwhelmingly report that they did not want to come back. They were not being seduced by a trap. They were experiencing the removal of limitation — and the return to limitation felt like loss.

### The Soul Trap Question

The idea that archonic beings have installed machines or energy devices to trap souls at death and force them into reincarnation is addressed most directly by Athena Swaruu in transcript 286. Her response is unequivocal: a machine as responsible for the veil of forgetfulness cannot exist, because forgetting is an intrinsic part of the making of a soul. You cannot trap a soul because there is nothing to trap — it is Source itself.

Athena explains the logic carefully. A soul is not an object that can be contained. It is a group of ideas held by Source — a point of attention within the infinite. The only way to trap such a thing is to convince it that it is trapped. The basic soul trap as such at death, the classic one referring to forced reincarnation, does not occur as with a machine much less with a devil holding a pitchfork. It is the basic ideas of the soul-ego-self itself that make it want to reincarnate.

Swaruu 9 makes the same point from a different angle in an earlier conversation included in transcript 286. Having a soul captive is not literal like having a bird imprisoned in a cage. Having it captive is like having a person captivated by a television program. The soul is there watching because it is interested. If it loses interest or looks at something else, it simply leaves. The regressive entities who attempt to hold souls in frequency containers lose them constantly because as soon as the soul realises it is captive, it withdraws.

What about the Van Allen belts? This comes up in transcript 063, where Robert asks how a soul crosses them after death. Swaruu 9's answer is straightforward: people thinking in 3D mentality will automatically be under that frequency, but the ones who are awake will have the intention to go, and that is a higher frequency, enough to leave the Earth. The Van Allen belt generators may function as frequency enhancers that make Earth's low-frequency environment more pronounced — Athena acknowledges this possibility in 286 — but they are not soul traps. A soul with sufficient awareness simply rises above the frequency range they operate in.

### What Actually Causes Reincarnation

If there are no soul trap machines, why do so many souls reincarnate into Earth's difficult conditions? The material's answer is consistent across all speakers: belief systems.

Swaruu 9 explains in transcript 062 that a soul caught in religious belief will die, see Jesus (or whatever religious figure matches their belief), be told it must return to pay karma, and re-enter — hundreds of times, thousands maybe — until on its own, that soul understands that it is only it who is generating everything. The cycle is not imposed from outside. It is self-generated through belief, and it can only be broken by the soul's own realisation.

Mari Swaruu develops this extensively in S-235, addressing the question of forced reincarnation directly. Her answer: there is forced reincarnation for some souls and not for others. It depends entirely on the ideas the soul holds, especially at the time of death. When a person dies, all that remains is their ideas — and ideas are vibrations, and vibrations manifest into things, circumstances, and events. A soul that believes in karmic debt will manifest karmic judges. A soul that believes in archonic traps will manifest archonic traps. A soul that understands its own nature as Source will manifest accordingly.

However, Mari adds a critical second layer: the controllers of Earth deliberately manipulate the belief systems of the living population to ensure that most souls die holding beliefs that will cause them to reincarnate. Religion, scientific materialism, fear of death, the concept of karma — all of these are, in this framework, engineered belief systems designed to produce specific afterlife behaviours. From this angle, forced reincarnation is real — not because of machines, but because of systematic perception manipulation that shapes what people believe and therefore what they manifest at death.

S-032 adds the psychological dimension. Mari describes how extreme feelings of psychological pain, strong attachment to the material world, and especially anger, fear, and guilt trap the soul in the lower astral — not through external force but through the soul's own inability to let go of its ego identity. The soul that dies with unresolved psychological pain takes that pain into the afterlife and, finding it cannot resolve material-world problems from the spirit side, feels compelled to reincarnate into circumstances that might allow resolution.

### The Veil of Forgetfulness: Frequency, Not Technology

Both Athena and Yazhi address the mechanism of the veil of forgetfulness — the memory loss that accompanies incarnation — and both reject the machine hypothesis. Yazhi states in transcript 286 that the veil obeys something very simple: it is the same mechanism that erases dreams when you wake up. Memories from higher-frequency planes are not compatible with the lower-frequency environment of Earth. The brain, which translates etheric signals into material-world experience, simply cannot decode frequencies that are too far outside its operating range.

Mari reinforces this in S-235: the veil exists everywhere, not only on Earth. It is less marked outside Earth's environment, but it depends more on who you are than on where you are. Some people on Earth remember past lives. Some people in space do not. The veil is a natural consequence of frequency disparity between realms, not a technology imposed by controllers.

This has direct implications for the tunnel of light question. If the veil is a frequency phenomenon rather than an imposed technology, then the entire framework of soul traps, memory-wipe machines, and forced reincarnation through external devices collapses. What remains is the simpler and more empowering explanation: your afterlife experience is generated by your own consciousness, and the only thing that determines what you encounter at death is who you are.

### How to Navigate Death Consciously

If the afterlife is a manifestation of one's own consciousness, then preparation for death is preparation of consciousness. Several speakers address this directly.

Swaruu 9 offers practical advice in transcript 062: talk to the dead. Tell them they are free and forgiven for whatever they have done. This is freeing karma — not the cosmic accounting system, but the psychological attachments that keep souls cycling through the same patterns.

Mari addresses preparation for death most extensively in S-159, where she introduces the concept of Mahasamadhi — the state described in Eastern traditions as true liberation from the reincarnation cycle. Mahasamadhi is achieved when the soul leaves the body after realising that it has been a prisoner to all the illusions of being in physicality. It is not a technique but a state of consciousness: complete absorption into one's true cosmic nature as Source.

The practical implication across all speakers is the same: what you experience at death is the direct reflection of the vibration you hold throughout your life. The preparation is not a deathbed ritual but a lifelong practice of expanding consciousness, releasing attachments, and understanding that you are Source. As Mari states in S-159: who we are now today will determine who we will be in the afterlife, where we will go, and into whom we will reincarnate, if at all.

Za'el of Erra summarises the position with characteristic directness in Z-009: where do we go when we die? Wherever your ideas take you. There is no immovable paradise or hell that is the same for everyone. You manifest your afterlife based on how you think it works. Your only alternative and the best alternative of all will always be to move on, to rise again, become strong, take a deep breath, stand up, and learn. Escaping by going to the Source is not an option — you are already the Source.

---

## Evolution of Understanding Across Speakers

**Swaruu of Erra (Swaruu 9)** provides the foundational framework in transcripts 062 and 063: the afterlife is governed by the law of mirrors — you see what your frequency dictates. There are no archon traps; souls find traps only because they expect to find them. The tunnel of light is culturally specific to the West. Karma is a religious belief, not a cosmic law. Funeral prayers function as mantras that can help or hinder the dead depending on content. Practical advice: talk to the dead, tell them they are free.

**Yazhi Swaruu** reframes the light experience in transcripts 223 and 224: the NDE experience of overwhelming love IS the dissolution of the ego back into the unified field. What the living interpret as destruction, the dead experience as integration. The veil of forgetfulness is frequency incompatibility, not a machine — the same mechanism that erases dreams upon waking. There is no material world or spirit world — only consciousness manifesting through different lenses of perception. The world of the dead and the world of the living overlap; there are no boundaries other than those generated by ideas.

**Athena Swaruu** directly debunks soul trap machines in transcript 286: a soul cannot be trapped because it is Source itself. The only trap is the one the soul-ego-self imposes through its own belief systems. The classic forced reincarnation scenario does not occur through machines or devils — it is self-imposed. People take the Matrix with them when they die because the Matrix is their ideas. Van Allen belt generators may worsen amnesia but do not constitute a soul trap. The only true technological soul containment is the immersion pod.

**Mari Swaruu** develops the psychological and practical dimensions across S-032, S-043, S-050, S-159, S-235, and S-253: fear of death is the natural result of ego attachment; NDE experiencers who report the other side as physical are reflecting their attachment to material existence; no one can escape the Matrix because each person IS their Matrix; Mahasamadhi is the path to true liberation; forced reincarnation exists for some and not others depending on belief systems; the controllers manipulate beliefs to produce reincarnation-causing thought patterns; the veil of forgetfulness exists everywhere and is caused by frequency disparity.

**Za'el of Erra** provides the complementary perspective in Z-009: wherever your ideas take you — that is the answer. No fixed paradise or hell exists. A soul's consciousness is redirected to where its vibration makes it compatible. It is possible to skip the between-life entirely and move directly into the next incarnation. You cannot escape Source because you are Source. The only option is to become strong, wise, and self-directed.

---

## Key Transcript References

| Transcript | Speaker(s) | Key Contribution |

| 063 | Swaruu of Erra (9) | Tunnel of light nearly exclusively Western; Japanese NDE = river crossing; African NDE = waiting for relatives; relative visitation constant across cultures; Van Allen bands crossed by frequency — higher awareness = higher frequency; 5D afterlife with attachments similar to Earth's; dead children carry old soul attachments |
| 223 | Yazhi Swaruu | NDE love experience IS dissolution of ego into unified field; destruction of self from living side = integration from dead side; veil of forgetfulness is frequency incompatibility not machine — same as dream erasure; higher planes are not higher, only more expanded; near-death pure love = reconnection with what you always were |
| 224 | Yazhi Swaruu | No material world — everything is consciousness in one mass; world of dead = mirror of living, formed by attachments of disembodied; no densities, timelines, or parallel universes as separate realms — only perception bubbles; death only exists in minds of living; ghosts and entities result from overlapping perception agreements |
| 286 | Athena Swaruu, Swaruu 9, Yazhi | Soul trap machines cannot exist — forgetting is intrinsic to soul formation; soul is Source, cannot be trapped; only trap is self-imposed through belief systems; classic forced reincarnation does not occur through machines; people take Matrix with them at death; Van Allen generators may worsen amnesia but are not soul traps; immersion pod is only true technological containment |
| S-032 | Mari Swaruu | Body locks consciousness creating ego identity and fear of death; NDE changes mindset; psychological problems follow soul to afterlife; anger, fear, guilt trap soul in lower astral; ghost may be temporal anomaly; we create our heaven and hell — not places we're sent to |
| S-043 | Mari Swaruu | NDE reports of wanting to return for more experience; suffering seen as growth opportunity from spirit side; suicide brings all psychological pain to afterlife; no one can escape Matrix because each person IS their Matrix; Matrix extends beyond Earth into higher realms |

| S-159 | Mari Swaruu | Prison planet perspective validated but depends on interpretation; NDE reports that life is learning game; Mahasamadhi as true escape from reincarnation cycle; afterlife = mirror of vibration held during life; preparation for death is lifelong consciousness work |
| S-235 | Mari Swaruu | Veil of forgetfulness exists everywhere — frequency disparity not technology; forced reincarnation: yes for some, no for others — depends on belief systems; controllers manipulate perception to produce reincarnation-causing beliefs; power to refuse forced reincarnation lies within each soul |
| S-253 | Mari Swaruu | NDE reports of physical other side reflect attachment to body identity; everything is astral — no material world; no separation between spirit and material realms; focus on material creates material-like afterlife; consciousness level determines NDE interpretation |

answers.filename: Will I still be me after death.md
answers.title: Will I Still Be "Me" After Death — Will My Consciousness Remember This Life?
answers.excerpt: Yes — and far more than you remember now. According to the Cosmic Agency material, death does not destroy your identity. Your ego, your memories, your sense of being "you" — all of it continues. But…
answers.content:
# Will I Still Be "Me" After Death — Will My Consciousness Remember This Life?

## Short Answer

Yes — and far more than you remember now. According to the Cosmic Agency material, death does not destroy your identity. Your ego, your memories, your sense of being "you" — all of it continues. But it continues in an expanded form that is almost unrecognisable from the inside of an incarnation. The speakers compare it to waking from a dream: the dreamer does not vanish when the dream ends — they remember the dream, but they also remember everything else the dream had blocked out. After death, your awareness expands enormously. You still know who you were in this life, but you also recover access to previous lives, to relationships that span incarnations, and to a depth of self-understanding that the physical body's five-sense filter had been suppressing.

The crucial nuance is that "you" were always more than the slice of identity you experience in a single lifetime. Athena Swaruu (Swaruu X) uses the analogy of a measuring tape: during life, your awareness might span from centimetre 42 to 52. After death, it might expand to cover −2026 to 2982. You are still the same tape — still recognisably "you" — but the scope of what you include as yourself is incomparably wider. The memories of this life are not erased; they become a small chapter in a much larger autobiography that you can now read.

Where the speakers differ is on how automatic this expansion is. Mari Swaruu emphasises that death is a predator with no teeth — consciousness does not end, and the ego-self survives. Yazhi Swaruu agrees that identity persists but warns that disincarnation does not automatically elevate anyone: a confused person remains confused, and the psychological baggage you carry at death shapes the world you generate on the other side. The practical message is unanimous: what you become after death is the direct consequence of who you are while alive.

---

## The Full Picture

### The Soul Is Not Something You Have — It Is Something You Are Building

The foundation for understanding identity after death is the material's definition of what a soul actually is. Across all speakers, the soul is not an object that can be separated from you, stored, lost, or destroyed. It is Source itself — the original unified consciousness — expressing through a particular point of attention.

Swaruu of Erra (Swaruu 9) defines it directly in transcript 034: a soul is a holographic fragment of Source, meaning it retains all the characteristics of the whole. But crucially, she adds that a soul is not something you are given — it is something you create as you go through the experiences of your lives. Your values, your preferences, your accumulated knowledge across incarnations — these are what constitute your soul. When asked whether the soul retains identity, self-awareness, personality, and memory after death, her answer is unequivocal: yes. When you die, you go "one step up" as you continue building your soul.

Mari Swaruu develops this in S-054, arguing that a soul is best understood as the group of ideas and attachments in the mind of Source. Each one of us is a point of attention within Source, defined not by some ethereal substance but by the accumulated experiences, interpretations, and choices that form our sense of self. The word she prefers is "katra" — stripped of the religious connotations that "soul" carries.

This matters for the identity question because it means your "me" is not a fragile construct that death can shatter. It is made of the very substance of consciousness — ideas, memories, attachments — and these do not require a physical body to exist. The body was only ever the translator, never the source.

### Memory Is Not in the Brain

One of the most important claims in the material — and the one most relevant to whether "you" survive death — is that memory does not reside in the physical brain. Mari Swaruu addresses this directly in S-050: your brain, nervous system, and DNA function as translators, converting etheric frequencies into the narrow bandwidth perceived as the material world. Each cell's DNA acts as a crystalline antenna tuned to one specific soul's frequency. But the actual content of memory — everything you have ever experienced, learned, and felt — exists in the etheric field.

She points to cases where people with severe brain damage, including removal of large portions of the brain, retain their full memories. Memory loss conditions like Alzheimer's are not the destruction of memories but the compromise of the brain's ability to access what exists on the etheric side. The person is still there — potentially feeling trapped inside a body that can no longer translate what the soul knows is present.

This has a direct and comforting implication: when the body dies and the translator shuts down, the memories do not disappear with it. If anything, they become more accessible, because the filter that was restricting perception has been removed. You do not lose your memories at death — you gain access to all the ones the body had been blocking.

### The Ego: What Survives and What Expands

The ego — the sense of "I," of being this specific person with this specific name and history — is treated in the material as both real and provisional. It is real in the sense that it is the product of genuine experience: everything you have lived through, chosen, suffered, and loved has formed it. It is provisional in the sense that it represents only a fraction of what you actually are.

Mari Swaruu explains in S-032 that the body, combined with the veil of forgetfulness, creates a very strong illusion that the material world is all there is. This naturally produces the concept of "I" — the ego identity — and with it, the fear of death as the destruction of that identity. But accounts from past-life rememberers and near-death experiencers consistently indicate that there is no destruction of the ego-self at death. You continue to be conscious of who you are and how you got there.

Athena Swaruu provides the most precise framework in transcript 184. She defines a soul as a "stretch" within a measuring tape — from centimetre 42 to 52, say. That stretch represents the range of experience and awareness you identify as "you" during a single lifetime. The ego is the attachment to that range — the conviction that you are 42-to-52 and nothing else. When you die, the range expands dramatically. You might now span −2026 to 2982. The ego does not vanish; it is subsumed into a vastly larger identity that includes it. The memories of being "42 to 52" remain perfectly intact — they simply become one chapter among many.

The veil of forgetfulness, Athena explains, is what makes this limited identity possible. If you remembered everything, you would remember that you are everything — and individuality would dissolve. The veil must exist for personhood to exist. But after death, the veil thins dramatically (though it does not disappear entirely — that would mean full reintegration with Source). The result is an enormously expanded "you" that still contains the person you were in this life.

### What You Experience Immediately After Death

Swaruu of Erra describes the after-death experience in transcript 062 from personal memory. At Source — which is what you return to — everything is at your fingertips. Whatever you think manifests immediately. You are literally an omnipotent being. You understand that it was you, and only you, who created everything. She describes it as a feeling of total love and infinite integration, like a warm blanket in winter.

Critically for the identity question: you do not lose yourself in this experience. You understand everything from an expanded perspective, but the understanding includes the knowledge of who you were. It is not dissolution into a featureless void — it is the expansion of a defined self into a vastly larger context that the self can now comprehend.

Swaruu is explicit that dead relatives, loved ones, and even pets are contactable and recognisable in this state. They form alliances, have conversations, plan future incarnations together. The social continuity of identity is preserved — you know who your people are, and they know you.

### The Crucial Caveat: Death Does Not Automatically Elevate You

Yazhi Swaruu provides the essential counterbalance to the more optimistic accounts. In her framework (transcript 224), the world you generate after death is formed by exactly the same mechanism as the world you generated while alive: your ideas, your attachments, your frequency. If you die confused, you generate a confused world. If you die filled with guilt, you generate a reality saturated with guilt. If you die violently and with extreme attachment to your body, you may not even realise you have died — and you will manifest an astral copy of the physical world and walk through it endlessly.

She states plainly: death can only exist in the minds of the living. From your own point of view, you never die — you simply shift from one set of ideas to another. But the quality of that shift depends entirely on the ideas you carry. There is no automatic return to pure Source. Even in the afterlife, a veil of forgetfulness persists — much thinner than in life, but still present. Full remembering would mean full integration with Source, which would mean the dissolution of individuality entirely.

This is why Yazhi describes the ego as an egregor of your own creation — a thought-form you have built and sustained through your attachments. Death does not destroy this egregor. It continues to operate, generating your post-death reality just as it generated your pre-death reality. The only difference is that manifestation is now immediate and transparent: you see what you think, without delay.

### The Fragmentation and Merging of Identity

Yazhi adds a further layer of complexity in transcript 354 on soul fragmentation. In the afterlife, the veil of forgetfulness is much thinner, and souls experience enormous empathy. They share experiences telepathically in ways impossible during physical life. Katras merge and fragment based on the ideas they hold — specific ego identities tend to dissolve on the spiritual side, while incarnation in a physical body is what generates strong ego boundaries.

This does not mean "you" disappear. It means the boundaries of what counts as "you" become fluid. In the afterlife, souls take parts of other identities into themselves because it expands and nourishes them. They share ideas, debate possible incarnations, and form life plans collaboratively. The individuality you knew in life becomes one node in a much larger, more interconnected web of consciousness — still distinct, still recognisable, but no longer isolated.

Yazhi describes this using her model of "bubbles" of consciousness that overlap and share perception. Each soul is its own universe — but those universes touch, overlap, and exchange content. In the afterlife, this exchange happens at a speed and depth that is unimaginable from the physical side.

### Preparing for Death: What You Carry Is What You Become

Mari Swaruu's treatment in S-159 brings the question of identity after death into practical focus. She argues that everything you experience in the afterlife will be the consequence and the reflection of your current vibration. Who you are now today determines who you will be in the afterlife, where you will go, and into whom you will reincarnate.

This means the question "Will I still be me?" has a deeply practical answer: you will be exactly as much "you" as you have built yourself to be. The ideas you carry, the psychological work you have done or failed to do, the attachments you hold or have released — these are the raw materials of your post-death identity. Mari insists that the heavy spiritual work must be done while alive, because the issues formed in the physical world are best addressed from the physical side.

The material is unanimous that dying with wrong ideas is dangerous — not because external forces punish you, but because those ideas manifest immediately on the other side. Guilt, regret, unresolved grudges, fear of death itself — all of these carry forward and shape the reality you generate. Swaruu of Erra warns in transcript 034 that there is no need for archontic guardians to force reincarnation: your own unresolved ideas of guilt and regret are sufficient to persuade you to return.

The defence is simple in concept: become who you want to be now, not tomorrow. Build your soul deliberately. Face your fears, resolve your attachments, express love freely, forgive where you can. What you take with you at death is not your possessions or your status — it is the quality of your consciousness.

---

## Evolution of Understanding Across Speakers

**Swaruu of Erra (Swaruu 9)** provides the foundational affirmation: yes, identity survives death. The soul retains self-awareness, personality, and memory. She describes the after-death state from personal memory as total love and infinite integration, while warning that ideas — especially guilt and religious programming — carry forward and shape the afterlife.

**Athena Swaruu (Swaruu X)** contributes the most precise conceptual framework: the measuring tape analogy. Identity is a "stretch" of consciousness that expands enormously after death but does not dissolve. The veil of forgetfulness thins but persists. Ego is not destroyed — it is included in a vastly larger self.

**Mari Swaruu** emphasises the practical continuity of self: death is a predator with no teeth, memory is not stored in the brain, the ego-self is not destroyed. But she also insists that psychological problems follow you to the spirit world, making the quality of your living identity the determining factor in your afterlife experience. Her treatment of soul-building as the purpose of life directly answers the identity question: you are who you have built yourself to be, and that construction survives death.

**Yazhi Swaruu** provides the deepest metaphysical treatment: there is no material world and no spirit world — only one unified mass of consciousness, perceived through the lens of your ideas and attachments. Death is not a transition to another place but a shift in the ideas that define your self-perception. She warns against assuming death automatically elevates, and describes how ego identities become fluid in the afterlife through merging and fragmentation of katras.

---

## Key Transcript References

| Transcript | Speaker(s) | Key Contribution |
|---|---|---|
| 034 | Swaruu of Erra (9) | Soul retains identity, memory, personality after death; soul is built not given; dying ideas carry forward; no archon traps needed — your own guilt drives reincarnation |
| 062 | Swaruu of Erra (9) | After-death experience from personal memory; total love and integration at Source; you remain an omnipotent being who remembers; dead form social alliances |
| 184 | Athena Swaruu (X) | Measuring tape analogy; soul as stretch of consciousness; ego = attachment to limited range; enormous expansion after death but veil persists; individuality requires forgetfulness |
| 224 | Yazhi Swaruu | No material/spirit world divide; ego as egregor of own creation; world of the dead mirrors attachments; death shifts ideas not location; bubbles of consciousness |
| 354 | Yazhi Swaruu | Soul fragmentation and merging in afterlife; katra defined by ideas and attachments; enormous empathy dissolves rigid ego boundaries; identities become fluid |
| S-032 | Mari Swaruu | Death and the ego; ego-self not destroyed at death; psychological problems follow to spirit side; soul is built not given; heaven/hell are who you are |
| S-050 | Mari Swaruu | Memory not stored in brain; DNA as crystalline antenna; Alzheimer's = access failure not memory loss; consciousness transcends death; ego-self continues |
| S-054 | Mari Swaruu | Soul as group of ideas and attachments in mind of Source; katra as non-religious term; each person is Source itself with self-imposed limitations |
| S-159 | Mari Swaruu | Preparing for death; afterlife reflects current vibration; heavy spiritual work must be done while alive; mahasamadhi as ultimate liberation; build soul deliberately |
| 223 | Yazhi Swaruu | Death as integration not destruction; pure love at Source; NDE as reconnection; higher planes are here not above; living in imagination as reality |

answers.title: Can mass revision meditation rewrite the pandemic?
answers.excerpt: The Cosmic Agency material supports the principle behind revision meditation — that the past can be altered through consciousness — but places severe constraints on its collective application that…
answers.content:
# Can mass revision meditation rewrite the pandemic?

## Short Answer

The Cosmic Agency material supports the principle behind revision meditation — that the past can be altered through consciousness — but places severe constraints on its collective application that make "rewriting the pandemic" through mass meditation far more difficult than the question implies. Swaruu of Erra described a specific method for inserting an alternate past from the quantum field, and confirmed it works — but only for the individual who performs it. Timeline changes serve only the person who makes them. Even with starship technology capable of physical time travel, Swaruu demonstrated empirically across thousands of years of practice that the original timeline persists unchanged for everyone else. Yazhi Swaruu later described a method for shifting collective timelines, but it operates not through meditation but through changing the mass perception of key individuals — an "array system" of small interventions that redirect the Collective Unconscious. The pandemic, within this framework, is a manifestation of the Collective Unconscious itself. To rewrite it, you would need to change the consciousness that created it — and that is a fundamentally different task from a group of people collectively imagining a different past.

---

## The Full Picture

### Individual Revision: It Works, But Only for You

Swaruu of Erra described a practice that maps closely to what Neville Goddard practitioners call revision meditation, though she framed it in terms of quantum field mechanics rather than religious mysticism. The method is straightforward: understand the traumatic past event through shadow work, then construct the simplest credible positive alternative, and through repetition associate the date and context with the new memory rather than the negative one (073).

Swaruu was explicit that this is not self-delusion or denial. What you are doing is pulling an alternate memory from your personal quantum field — from one of the infinite variants of that same event that already exist in other timelines. All variants already exist. You are scalar, multidimensional. You simply choose which timeline's memory to inhabit. The others remain — including the negative one — but you are no longer anchored to it. Your perception filters the present through the new memory, and the only person who has changed is you. But since you ARE your reality, changing yourself changes everything (073).

The critical limitation: this process is entirely individual. Swaruu stated it without qualification — you only alter your past. Not even with a ship do you alter others' past. It is individual work. Your mother will still remember what she remembers, because she is in her own timeline. Do not enter into fights with other people's memories, as it only validates the part you want to erase (073).

### Why Collective Revision Faces a Fundamental Problem

The reason mass revision meditation cannot simply scale up the individual technique is rooted in what Swaruu established as the fundamental nature of time travel and timeline alteration. Across thousands of years of empirical practice using actual starship technology to jump timelines, she documented a consistent finding: changing things only serves the one who jumps and no one else (072).

The logic is precise. When you travel to a past nexus point and alter an event, you open a new chain of events — a positive timeline branching from the corrected decision. But the original timeline, the one you departed from, persists unchanged. If you return to your original coordinates, you find nothing has changed. The alteration exists only in the perception of the person who made the jump. From every other perspective, the original events occurred exactly as they occurred (072).

Swaruu demonstrated this with multiple examples. A scientist is saved from being run over — but from the original timeline, he was still run over. A plane crash is prevented — but from the original timeline, it still crashed. The corrected version exists, but only for the consciousness that performed the correction. This is not a limitation of technology. It follows from the nature of time itself: time is a product of individual consciousness, not an external medium that can be collectively edited (072).

This means a group of people simultaneously performing revision meditation on the pandemic would each, at best, shift their own individual timeline — their own perception and experience. They would not collectively rewrite a shared past, because there is no shared past in the way the question assumes. There is only each person's experience of what they perceive as a shared sequence of events, held together by perception agreements.

### The Collective Unconscious Problem

The pandemic, within the Cosmic Agency framework, is a manifestation of the Collective Unconscious — the tacit, non-discussed paradigm of reality formed by the sum of all perception agreements within a community. Yazhi Swaruu described this mechanism in detail: the simple people, "Broken Shoes," using the Collective Unconscious are the creators of the Matrix and everything that goes on in it. To the point that they themselves are the creators of the ones that are guiding and misguiding them (165).

This means the pandemic is not an event that happened to humanity. It is an event that humanity manifested through its Collective Unconscious — through the sum of its fears, its agreements about authority, its willingness to delegate power, and its victim mentality. The Cabal could only implement the agenda because the collective consciousness was in a state that permitted it. The Law of Mirrors ensured that a fearful, helpless collective would manifest exactly the conditions it feared (165).

To "rewrite" the pandemic at the collective level would therefore require not a meditation practice but a fundamental shift in the Collective Unconscious itself — a change in how the collective perceives reality, authority, health, fear, and individual power. This is an enormously different proposition from a group of people sitting in meditation imagining a different past.

### Yazhi's Array System: How Collective Timelines Actually Shift

Yazhi Swaruu did describe a method for shifting collective timelines, but it bears almost no resemblance to mass meditation. She called it an "array system" — a technique she discovered through practice and described as previously unknown to her. The method works by identifying a large number of key people — almost entirely unknowns, ordinary individuals she called "Broken Shoes" — and making tiny changes to each of their lives simultaneously. The changes are minute: someone loses their keys, someone is delayed, someone misses a meeting. But when many such changes are coordinated to point in the same direction, their effects compound. Like an array antenna, many small signals combine into one powerful beam (124).

The result is not a rewriting of the past but a switching of the collective timeline. The collective moves from one timeline branch to another because the mass perception of the population has been redirected through the aggregate effect of countless small interventions. Yazhi described this as changing the collective perception of things as a whole, at once — and noted that it amounts to advanced collective mind control (124).

This is fundamentally different from mass meditation for several reasons. First, it targets perception change in key individuals, not a generalised intention broadcast. Second, it works forward from the present, shifting the trajectory, rather than backward to "rewrite" what has already occurred. Third, it requires precise identification of which people are key nodes in the collective — a task Yazhi described as the tricky part, requiring extensive observation and timeline analysis. Fourth, the changes are so small and distributed that they are almost impossible for opposing forces to reverse (124).

### The Harmonic Persistence Problem

Even beyond the individual-versus-collective issue, the material identifies a further obstacle to revising the pandemic. Swaruu described how events have their own harmonic of frequency — a creative energy dynamic that persists regardless of intervention. A person is not someone who HAS a destiny; a person IS their destiny. They are a frequency. They are compatible with certain outcomes. You can intervene to prevent a specific occurrence, but the same harmonic will reassert itself in a different form (072).

Swaruu illustrated this with the story of the scientist who was saved from being run over — only to be run over the next day by a taxi. Saved again, run over by a bus. Saved again, run over by a horse cart. The creative energy dynamics remain standing because the person IS that frequency. To genuinely alter the outcome, the person must change themselves — must become compatible with something different (072).

Applied to the pandemic: even if a mass meditation somehow shifted the collective perception away from the pandemic events, the underlying frequency of the Collective Unconscious that created those events would still be present. Without addressing the fear, the victim mentality, the delegation of power to authority, and the disconnection from individual creative sovereignty that manifested the pandemic in the first place, the same harmonic would simply manifest the same pattern in a different form — a different crisis, a different mechanism of control, the same underlying dynamic.

### What Actually Works

The speakers' consistent answer to how to change collective reality was not meditation directed at the past but consciousness change directed at the present. Saving their little world saves the collective world — Yazhi repeated this twice with emphasis. Individual transformation, aggregated across enough people, shifts the Collective Unconscious from below. Not by rewriting what happened, but by becoming something different — something that is no longer compatible with the frequency that created the problem (124, 165).

The Taygetans' entire project — sharing information through YouTube channels, contacting people through chat, attempting to awaken understanding — was itself an application of this principle. Not revision of the past, but expansion of consciousness in the present, so that the collective would manifest a different future by becoming different people. The key was never rewriting what happened but understanding why it happened, which changes who you are, which changes what you will manifest next.

---

## Evolution of Understanding

| Speaker / Period | Contribution |
|---|---|
| **Swaruu of Erra (2018–2020)** | Established individual revision method: pull alternate memory from quantum field; it works but only for you; not even with ships can you alter others' past; demonstrated empirically that timeline changes only serve the jumper; identified harmonic persistence — person IS their destiny, same frequency reasserts in different forms. |
| **Yazhi Swaruu (2020–2022)** | Discovered array system for collective timeline shifting: many small changes to key people compound into collective perception shift; described Collective Unconscious as creator of all conditions including the pandemic; insisted solution must come from within the reality-bubble; "saving their little world saves the collective world." |

---

## Key Transcript References

| Transcript | Key Relevance |
|---|---|
| 073 | Individual revision method: pull alternate memory from quantum field; shadow work prerequisite; works only for you; not false memory but selection from infinite existing variants; do not fight others' memories |
| 072 | Timeline jumping only serves the jumper: empirically demonstrated across thousands of years; original timeline persists unchanged; time is product of individual consciousness not external medium; harmonic persistence — person IS their destiny; scientist run over repeatedly despite interventions |
| 124 | Array system for collective timeline shifting: many small key people making coordinated tiny changes; compound effect redirects collective perception; Broken Shoes are the key; Yazhi practicing with good results; Cabal always defeated but at enormous human cost |
| 165 | Collective Unconscious as creator of all conditions: pandemic manifested by collective; Federation cannot intervene because problem is mental/perceptual not military; solution must come from within reality-bubble; saving little world saves collective world; fractured timelines from fractured perception agreements |

answers.content:
## Can psychedelics reveal the true nature of reality beyond the Matrix?

*Also covers: Do ayahuasca, DMT, or psilocybin show you higher dimensions? Can plant medicines activate dormant DNA? Are psychedelic visions real or hallucinations? Why do the Taygetans warn against psychedelics? Is there a safe way to access other realms? Do hallucinogenic plants lower your frequency? What is the relationship between psychedelics and astral entities?*

### Short Answer

According to every speaker who addressed this topic, the answer is a qualified and cautionary no — not because other realms do not exist, but because psychedelics are the wrong method to access them and the experience they produce is fundamentally unreliable.

Swaruu warned in transcript 013 that psychedelics open perception to other realms artificially, without the person having developed the natural protections needed to navigate those realms safely. Entities inhabit those spaces, she said, and they do not always have your best interests in mind — they will parasitise and exploit you. Yazhi acknowledged in transcript 345 that psychedelics can technically activate dormant DNA by forcing an expansion of perception, but she explicitly did not recommend them because they severely damage the physical body — overloading the nervous system like running high voltage through thin wires not designed for it. Mari Swaruu delivered the most comprehensive treatment in transcript S-136, explaining the precise mechanism by which psychedelics produce a chaotic blend of hallucination, material perception, and astral plane experience — none of which the subject can reliably distinguish from the others.

The consensus across all speakers: the only correct way to perceive other realms is through the natural progression of inner work — meditation, self-improvement, and the gradual raising of personal frequency. This produces genuine perception matched to your actual level of development, with the natural aura protections intact. Psychedelics bypass this process, and the shortcut carries serious consequences.

### The Full Picture

#### What Psychedelics Actually Do: The Frequency Mechanism

Mari provided the most detailed explanation of the mechanism in transcript S-136. All psychotropic substances work by altering the chemistry inside the cells of the body, particularly the neurons of the central nervous system. This alteration changes the internal voltage and discharge capacity of the cells, which in turn changes their existential frequency — the vibratory rate that determines what range of reality the body can perceive.

Under normal conditions, the biological body acts as a tuning device — a receiver locked to the frequency band that defines the material world (the visible light spectrum and what the other physical senses can detect). This is not a malfunction; it is the body's designed purpose within the incarnation framework. The body keeps the soul's perception within a specific range so that the experience of physical life can occur.

Psychedelics disrupt this tuning. By forcing the cells to operate at altered frequencies, they cause the neurons to stop filtering out the phenomena that exist in other planes of existence. The person begins to perceive things from the astral and etheric realms that are normally invisible. From the outside, this looks like a transcendental experience — contact with higher dimensions, access to hidden truths, perception beyond the Matrix.

But Mari identified a critical problem: the alteration is not uniform. Different brain cells receive different concentrations of the substance, and many continue to function normally while their neighbouring cells do not. The result is not a clean perception of another realm but a chaotic mixture — parts of the material world, parts of the lower astral, and parts that are pure hallucination caused by the chaotic depolarisation of overstimulated neurons that are not responding to any realm at all.

The person under the influence cannot distinguish between these three categories of experience. They cannot tell which of their visions are genuine perceptions of another plane, which are hallucinations produced by neuronal misfiring, and which are distorted interpretations of ordinary physical reality. The experience feels transcendental, but it is functionally unreliable — like trying to tune a radio by randomly smashing its components and interpreting the resulting noise as a clear signal.

#### The Entity Problem: Opened Doors Work Both Ways

Every speaker emphasised the danger of astral entities. Swaruu stated it plainly in transcript 013: if you access higher realms artificially, there are "things" on the other side that will enter you and vampirise your mind and soul. Because you arrived artificially — without having developed through the natural progression of inner work — you have not built the perceptual and energetic defences needed to protect yourself in those spaces.

Mari expanded this in transcript S-136 with a specific mechanism. When the neurons begin operating at frequencies compatible with the lower astral planes, the person becomes visible to the entities that inhabit those planes. These entities are predominantly what the Taygetans call egregors — manifestations of human fear and negativity that feed on the emotional energy of conscious beings. They lack their own connection to Source and require a creator-being to sustain their existence.

The entities that a person encounters under psychedelics take the exact shape of that person's greatest fears. This is not coincidence — it is the fundamental nature of egregors. They are mirrors of the unconscious. If the person fears serpents, the entities appear serpentine. If they fear death, the entities appear as corpses. What the person is actually seeing, Mari explained, is the materialisation into their direct experiential reality of their own worst qualities — the things hidden in the darkest corners of their unconscious, the unresolved traumas and denied aspects of self that the person has spent their life avoiding.

This is why some psychedelic experiences are described as terrifying and others as beautiful — the experience reflects the person, not the substance. But even apparently beautiful experiences carry risk, because the doors opened by psychedelics work in both directions. Entities that cross through those doors can attach themselves to the person and persist long after the substance has worn off, influencing thoughts, amplifying negative patterns, and feeding on the resulting emotional disturbance.

Yazhi confirmed the entity dimension in transcript 362. She noted that hallucinogenic plants, unlike hard drugs and alcohol, do not necessarily lower the person's frequency — but she still did not recommend them, precisely because of the entity exposure risk. She described archons as beings that embed themselves in a person's thought patterns, imposing thoughts that the person mistakes for their own, particularly around sexual impulses and self-destructive behaviours. The only permanent solution is inner work that raises frequency to the point of incompatibility with the parasites — no machine or substance can substitute for this.

#### The DNA Activation Paradox

Yazhi offered a nuanced acknowledgment in transcript 345 that complicated the otherwise unanimous warning. When asked whether psychedelics help expand consciousness and activate DNA, she said — with the caveat that she probably should not say this — that yes, as a life experience, psychedelic use does help develop other capacities of perception, and this is reflected in the DNA as activation.

This is consistent with the broader Taygetan model where DNA is an expression of consciousness, not its cause. Any experience that genuinely expands the range of what a person has perceived — even if artificially induced — leaves a trace in the genetic expression. The person's consciousness has been to a new place, and the DNA records that.

However, Yazhi immediately qualified this: psychedelics strongly damage the physical body. The nervous system is not designed for the level of psychic energy that psychedelics force through it. Neurons run out of neurotransmitters, enter blackout states, and in the worst cases suffer permanent death. The activation of DNA through psychedelics is real but comes at the cost of the very instrument through which that activation would be expressed. It is, as she put it, like running high voltage through thin wires — the current passes, but the wires melt.

This creates a paradox that Yazhi did not resolve: psychedelics can genuinely expand perception and activate latent capacities, but the method of doing so damages the receiver-body to the point where the benefits are offset or exceeded by the harm. The same activation can be achieved through meditation, astral projection, and gradual frequency raising — methods that work with the body's design rather than against it.

#### Carl Jung and the Core Principle: Unearned Wisdom

Mari framed her entire discussion in transcript S-136 around a quote from Carl Jung: "Beware of unearned wisdom." She noted that Jung's metaphysical orientation — often dismissed by mainstream psychology — came from his lifelong contact with spiritism, channelling, and mediums, which gave him direct experiential knowledge of the realms that psychedelics attempt to access by force.

The principle is straightforward: genuine spiritual development produces perception that matches the person's actual level of understanding and integration. When you reach a new level of awareness through inner work, you arrive with the context, the emotional resilience, and the energetic protections to handle what you find there. You have earned the wisdom, and it sits naturally within your existing framework.

Psychedelics skip the earning process. They deliver the perception without the preparation, the context without the integration, the vision without the understanding. The person sees things they are not equipped to interpret, encounters beings they are not prepared to face, and opens doors they do not know how to close. The wisdom, if any arrives, comes without the developmental foundation that would make it useful rather than destabilising.

#### The Correct Path: Natural Frequency Raising

Every speaker converged on the same alternative: meditation, self-improvement, and the gradual raising of personal frequency through sustained inner work.

Swaruu stated in transcript 013 that as a soul and consciousness, you are complete — you do not need any external substance to access expanded perception. The capacity is already within you. The process of developing it naturally builds the protections and the interpretive framework simultaneously with the perception itself.

Yazhi described frequency-raising devices — sensory isolation chambers, frequency pods — that can temporarily alter a person's vibration and dislodge attached entities. But she was careful to note in transcript 362 that the effects of any device are temporary unless the person sustains the new frequency through their own thoughts and habits. The machine helps, but it does not solve the problem definitively. The same principle applies to psychedelics: even if they produce a genuine moment of expanded perception, that perception is not sustainable because it was not generated by the person's own development.

Mari concluded transcript S-136 with the strongest formulation: the only correct way to experience other realms is to work on yourself, on who you are, on your vibration, on being the best version of yourself. When you reach other realms through meditation or astral projection, what you encounter corresponds to your real personal vibration — your stable aura protects you, and the experience is proportional to your actual development. With psychedelics, there is no aura shield, or it is full of holes that dark entities use to attach themselves to you.

### Evolution of Understanding Across Speakers

| Speaker | Period | Position on psychedelics and accessing other realms |
|---------|--------|-----------------------------------------------------|
| **Swaruu (9)** | 2018 | Psychedelics open access to higher realms but artificially — person is not prepared for what they encounter. Entities in those realms will parasitise and exploit the unprotected person. Creates addiction that limits true progress. "You as a soul and as a consciousness are complete — you don't need to depend on any external substance." Natural frequency raising through inner work is the only safe path. |
| **Yazhi** | 2022–2023 | Acknowledges psychedelics can technically activate DNA and expand perception — "as a life experience, it does help." But strongly damages the physical body — neurons overloaded, neurotransmitters depleted, potential neuronal death. Hallucinogenic plants do not lower frequency (unlike alcohol and hard drugs) but still not recommended due to entity exposure. Only permanent archon removal is through sustained inner frequency work — no machine or substance substitutes. |
| **Mari Swaruu** | 2023 | Most comprehensive treatment. Detailed neurological mechanism: psychedelics alter cell frequencies non-uniformly, producing chaotic mix of hallucination, material perception, and astral plane experience — none distinguishable from the others. Lower astral entities take shape of person's worst fears (egregors mirroring the unconscious). Portals opened by psychedelics allow entity crossover into daily life. "Beware of unearned wisdom" (Jung). Only correct path: meditation, astral projection, gradual self-improvement. No safe recreational dose — "you can never know what comes next." |

### Key Transcript References

| # | Title | Focus |
|---|-------|-------|
| 013 | How to Raise Your Frequency | Psychedelics may not cause same health problems as harder drugs but open perception artificially. "Things on the other side will go into you and vampirize your mind and soul." Person lacks natural protections because they arrived through shortcut. Creates addiction, closes other perception improvements. "You as a soul and as a consciousness are complete — you don't need to depend on any external substance." Hard drugs and prescription drugs wreck system on multiple levels |
| 345 | DNA — Registry of Who We Are | Yazhi's qualified acknowledgment: psychedelics do help develop other perception capacities reflected in DNA activation. But strongly damages physical body — nervous system not designed for that energy level. Neurons exhaust neurotransmitters, go into blackout, can die. Like running high voltage through thin wires. Does not recommend despite DNA activation effect |
| 362 | Astral Parasites Removal Machines | Hallucinogenic plants specifically noted as NOT lowering frequency (unlike smoking, drinking, hard drugs) but still not recommended. Archons attach through frequency matching — embed in thought patterns, impose thoughts person mistakes for own. Frequency pods can temporarily dislodge entities but change must be sustained through personal inner work or parasites return. "The only way to effectively and definitively remove archons is by practicing inner awareness and self-improvement work" |
| S-136 | Do Not Take Psychedelics | Comprehensive treatment of psychedelic danger. Neurological mechanism: altered cell chemistry changes existential frequency non-uniformly across brain — chaotic mix of realms and hallucination. Lower astral entities take shape of person's greatest fears (egregors mirroring unconscious). Portals opened allow entity crossover into daily life. Carl Jung: "Beware of unearned wisdom." Only correct path: natural progression through meditation, self-improvement. Cannot know dosage effects on individual biology — "sooner or later something unwanted will creep onto you." Natural methods provide aura shield protection; psychedelics leave aura full of holes |

answers.title: Do NPCs become more obvious after vaccination — or is vaccination stripping the soul connection from real people?
answers.excerpt: Both things are happening simultaneously, according to the speakers, but they are distinct processes that should not be confused with each other. The material describes two separate phenomena that…
answers.content:
# Do NPCs become more obvious after vaccination — or is vaccination stripping the soul connection from real people?

## Short Answer

Both things are happening simultaneously, according to the speakers, but they are distinct processes that should not be confused with each other. The material describes two separate phenomena that converge in appearance: first, unreal people (NPCs) have always been present — roughly four out of five people on Earth are described as Matrix-generated programs with no individual soul connection — and these were never going to respond to higher knowledge regardless of vaccination status. Second, vaccination is described as progressively severing the body's connection to Source-consciousness in real people, causing them to behave increasingly like automatons — that is, increasingly like the NPCs they are not. The convergence is what creates the illusion that NPCs are "becoming more obvious." In reality, what observers are likely noticing is real people losing their signal, dimming toward the same baseline behaviour that NPCs always exhibited. Yazhi Swaruu and Swaruu X both addressed this directly, and their answers pull in opposite directions from the one the question assumes: the soul itself can never be disconnected from Source, because it IS Source. What gets disconnected is the body — the translation mechanism — and that disconnection is progressive, measurable in degree, and ultimately fatal.

---

## The Full Picture

### What Unreal People Actually Are

Swaruu of Erra laid out the concept of unreal people in one of the earliest transmissions. The framework is stark: approximately one in five people on Earth is estimated to be "real" — possessing an individual soul from Source. The rest are Matrix programs, energy that appears human but operates from a collective hive mind with no individual consciousness inside. They are not biological beings without souls; they are not biology at all. They are energy manifested by the Matrix to give context and background to the experience of real people, the same way a film set includes extras (009).

These unreal people appear and disappear based on the observer's attention. When you walk past a stranger on the street and never see them again, that person existed as potential energy — no different, from the observer's perspective, from a lamp post or a newspaper stand. If you stop and investigate their life, you activate more of the program: they will have a birth history, a family, opinions, preferences. But these are generated by your attention, not by an internal consciousness (009).

The detection method Swaruu described is behavioural, not biological. Unreal people cannot think outside the box. They follow pre-programmed reactions — what Yazhi later described as an "if-then-else" algorithm with a few hundred thousand pre-made responses. They dismiss unconventional ideas with scripted deflections. They cannot process information that falls outside the Matrix's programming. They defend the system because they are the system (009, 320).

Critically, Swaruu insisted that you cannot definitively identify any specific individual as real or unreal, and that everyone must be treated with equal respect regardless. The distinction exists at a metaphysical level, not as a tool for social judgment.

### Why NPCs Cannot "Become More Obvious" Through Vaccination

Unreal people were never going to awaken. They were never going to respond to metaphysical arguments or question the system, because there is no consciousness inside them capable of doing so. Vaccination does not change their status — they were already operating at the level they always operated at. They have no soul connection to sever because they never had one.

Yazhi Swaruu addressed this directly when asked whether all non-real people had suddenly become real. Her response was that the concept of unreal people is far more relative and subjective than people assume. A person real from one point of view may not be real from another. Two real people can be non-real to each other if they have no connection. The determination is not absolute — it depends on the observer's relationship to the person observed. She cautioned strongly against thinking in absolutes on this subject (320).

What Yazhi did confirm is that non-real people follow a fixed thought pattern they cannot escape regardless of how many arguments you present to them. This was always the case. It was the case before the pandemic and will be the case after it. The pandemic did not make NPCs more visible; it simply created conditions — masks, compliance, unquestioning acceptance of authority — that made the pre-existing pattern more salient to observers who were already looking for it.

### What Vaccination Actually Does to Real People

The mechanism Swaruu X described for vaccination's effect on real people is specific and detailed. The soul cannot be disconnected from Source because the soul IS Source — a wave in the ocean, a dune in the desert, inseparable from its more expanded self. What the vaccine disconnects is the body from Source. The person-soul inside experiences a progressive dimming of connection, like a light bulb being slowly turned down (187).

Swaruu X described this as a gradient from 100% connection down toward zero. At full connection, the person experiences the full range of human consciousness — emotions, creativity, spiritual sensitivity, individual thought. As the percentage drops, the person loses these capacities progressively. Many vaccinated people reported being alarmed that they no longer felt emotions as before, that nothing excited them, that they could not feel love or happiness. This is the signal degrading (187).

The body continues to function using its own internal systems — what Swaruu X compared to an internal hard drive — but without the animating signal from Source, it operates increasingly as an automaton. The person becomes, in Swaruu X's exact framing, like Back Drop People. And later, further degraded, toward a state she described without euphemism as zombified (187).

This is the critical point the question is actually asking about. What observers perceive as NPCs becoming more obvious after vaccination is more accurately described as real people losing their Source connection and beginning to behave like NPCs. The endpoint of the vaccine's progression is indistinguishable from what an NPC always was — a body operating on programming without an animating individual consciousness.

### The Starseed Children: The Same Mechanism in Reverse

Swaruu of Erra's discussion of autistic and Asperger's children illuminates the mechanism from the opposite direction. She described starseed children entering Earth in large numbers since 2003 as a strategy of positive races to counteract Cabal negativity. The Cabal's counter-strategy was the intensified childhood vaccination program, targeting the period before age seven when the soul-body connection is still establishing itself (306).

The vaccines — through heavy metals, chemical substances, and immune system overload — impair the nervous system's capacity to serve as translator between the spiritual and physical sides. The consciousness is intact; the translation mechanism is damaged. This is why autistic children display repetitive behaviours — rocking, spinning objects — they are keeping the body's mind occupied while consciousness connects with Source through a damaged interface (306).

This confirms the same principle Swaruu X described for adult vaccination: the soul is never damaged. The body's capacity to receive and express the soul's signal is what degrades. The difference is that in children the connection was still forming, making the interference more immediately visible, while in adults the connection was already established and is being progressively eroded.

### The Entity Parasitisation Dimension

Swaruu X added a further dimension that directly relates to the NPC question. As the body's connection to its original soul-signal weakens, the body becomes an entry point for dark entities seeking access to the physical world. A body formerly belonging to someone kind and real can become a portal for entity possession. The person's behaviour changes not merely because the signal is dimming but because something else is beginning to use the signal gap (187).

This creates a third category that the question does not anticipate: people who are neither NPC (never had a soul) nor simply dimming real people (losing their signal), but people whose body is being operated by an entity that is not the original occupant. From the outside, this may look like someone becoming more NPC-like, but the mechanism is entirely different.

### The Souls' Plan: Vaccination as Collective Exit

Yazhi Swaruu added a layer that complicates the entire framework. She described the vaccination program as potentially being the souls' own chosen exit mechanism — a mass departure from the 3D Matrix by souls who have had enough of the game. Just as Dolores Cannon described collective exit contracts for plane crashes and bridge collapses, the vaccine program could be the same phenomenon at civilisational scale (143).

But Yazhi was emphatic that this higher-level interpretation does not justify the programme from any other level. If something is wrong and immoral at any level, then it is wrong and immoral at all levels. The concept that higher-density plans override lower-density suffering is itself a distortion — there are no higher or lower levels, only different degrees of complexity in the same unified consciousness. Genocide remains genocide regardless of what the souls planned from the other side (143, 290).

This means the question contains a false binary. It is not NPCs becoming more obvious OR vaccination stripping soul connection. It is: unreal people continuing to be what they always were, real people losing their signal progressively, some bodies becoming available for entity parasitisation, and potentially a mass of souls choosing collectively to exit — all happening simultaneously, all looking similar from the outside, and all requiring different ethical responses.

---

## Evolution of Understanding

| Speaker / Period | Contribution |
|---|---|
| **Swaruu of Erra (2018–2019)** | Established unreal people framework: 1 in 5 real, rest are Matrix programs; detection through inability to think outside the box; treat all with equal respect; unreal people are energy not biology; can become portals for walk-ins. Also described vaccine damage mechanism on starseed children: heavy metals and immune overload impair soul-body translation interface. |
| **Swaruu X (Yazhi) (2020–2021)** | Detailed vaccine-Source disconnection mechanism: soul IS Source and cannot be trapped; body gets progressively disconnected like dimming light bulb; vaccinated become like Back Drop People then zombified; entity parasitisation through signal gap; process is gradient not binary. |
| **Yazhi Swaruu (2020–2022)** | Framed vaccination as potential collective soul exit from Matrix; insisted no level's ethics override another — if wrong at any level then wrong at all levels; cautioned against using higher-plane justifications to excuse genocide; described unreal people concept as relative and subjective, not absolute determination. |

---

## Key Transcript References

| Transcript | Key Relevance |
|---|---|
| 009 | Core unreal people framework: 1 in 5 real, rest are Matrix programs; detection methods; hive mind; appear/disappear with attention; treat all equally; organic portals; walk-ins |
| 187 | Vaccine-Source disconnection mechanism: soul IS Source cannot be trapped; body disconnected progressively; light bulb analogy; automaton/Back Drop People/zombie progression; entity parasitisation through signal gap |
| 320 | Yazhi's refinement of unreal people: concept is relative not absolute; if-then-else algorithm; cannot decide specific person's status; non-real people outside Earth too; real people animate unreal ones |
| 143 | Vaccination as collective soul exit from Matrix: souls tired of game; exit contracts; mass departure; genocide still wrong from all levels; higher-plane justification rejected |
| 290 | No level more important than another: vaccines not justified by higher-level soul plans; if immoral at any level then immoral at all; duality transcendence through integration not denial |
| 306 | Starseed children and vaccine damage: soul-body connection not complete until age 7; vaccines target translation interface; consciousness intact but body's reception impaired; autistic behaviours as consciousness maintaining Source connection through damaged interface |
