answers.title: Why does the Federation restrict help to Earth when starseeds from its own member races are incarnated and suffering there?
answers.excerpt: This is one of the sharpest contradictions in the entire Cosmic Agency material, and every speaker who touches it arrives at it from a different angle — but none of them resolve it comfortably. The…
answers.content:
## Q27: Why does the Federation restrict help to Earth when starseeds from its own member races are incarnated and suffering there?

### Short Answer

This is one of the sharpest contradictions in the entire Cosmic Agency material, and every speaker who touches it arrives at it from a different angle — but none of them resolve it comfortably. The Federation's own member races sent their people into Earth as starseeds. Those starseeds are now trapped inside a system the Federation claims it cannot or will not interfere with. And the Federation actively restricts the very races who sent them from helping them once they are there.

The official Federation position, as described across multiple transcripts, is that starseeds agreed to the Earth experience before incarnating, that the Federation's role is to ensure they have the experience they came for, and that interference would violate the Prime Directive and undermine the soul growth that justified the incarnation in the first place. Yazhi offered a metaphysical explanation that goes deeper: the Federation literally cannot intervene because it does not exist within the human Collective Unconscious, and only starseeds — who hold one foot in each reality — can serve as bridges between the two. But this metaphysical framing sits uneasily beside the documented evidence that the Federation actively works against starseeds through its Cabal proxies, gives non-human technology to the controllers who attack them, and classifies regressive abductions as routine Matrix maintenance while threatening positive races for typing messages.

The Taygetan formal letter to the Federation, signed by Queen Alenym in January 2024, made the accusation explicit: the Federation is not merely failing to protect its own starseeds — it is "constantly intervening against them, encouraging the experience to be of greater pain and suffering." The starseed paradox is not a gap in the system. It is, as with so many Federation contradictions, a feature of it.

### The Full Picture

#### What Starseeds Are and How They Enter

The material describes three primary ways that non-human souls enter Earth. The first is the "crawl-in" — a soul physically born into a human body, what most people mean by "starseed." The second is the "step-in" — a soul exchange where one consciousness departs a healthy body and another takes over its life, memories, and identity. The third is the "step-down" — a full extraterrestrial who physically walks off a starship and into human society using a fabricated or inherited identity.

Beyond these, Athena described a fourth mechanism that accounts for the majority of modern starseeds: full immersion pods. These are technological devices, located on starships or on home planets, that transfer a soul's consciousness into a human body on Earth while the original body remains safely elsewhere. The starseed lives an entire human life — or a portion of one — as an avatar, with their real body maintained in stasis. As Athena explained it, the alliance of positive races that decided to help Earth did so primarily by sending starseeds in massive numbers, "knowing that most incarnate there using Immersion Pod technology, but not only, as many Step-Downs and Walk-ins and Source-born souls incarnate there with the agenda of awakening humanity and liberating it from oppression."

This means the starseed programme is not a passive spiritual phenomenon. It is an organised intervention by Federation member races — a deliberate strategy to change Earth from the inside. The children entering with "full memory," the waves of indigo and crystal children Swaruu of Erra described arriving since 2003, the step-downs who purchase groceries in human shops and drive cars on human roads — all of these represent a calculated deployment.

*Key sources: S-010 (three entry types, Mari), 225 (immersion pods and starseed cavalry, Athena), 306 (starseed children strategy since 2003, Swaruu 9)*

#### The Federation's Official Justification

The Federation's stated reason for not helping starseeds once they are on Earth rests on two pillars. The first is the Prime Directive — the same non-interference law that restricts all positive race activity regarding Earth. Since starseeds on Earth's surface are reclassified as human under Federation rules, they fall under the same restrictions as any other Earth inhabitant. Za'el addressed this directly: "For both Step Downs and starseeds, it has been strongly established that if they are there on Earth, they will be considered human beings in everything they say and do." He noted the fundamental incoherence — rules imposed "without any kind of coherence, especially knowing that it is set up this way by the United Federation of Planets, which does not make clear the rules that apply to being on or off the planet."

The second pillar is the soul agreement framework. The Federation claims that starseeds chose their Earth experience before incarnating, that the suffering they encounter is part of their growth plan, and that the Federation's role is merely to facilitate the experiences the souls demanded from higher planes. Mari described this position in her analysis of Federation politics: the Federation "claims it only moves things and events towards what the souls there demand, only facilitating those experiences that are already being created there."

But the Taygetan letter of January 2024 systematically dismantled both justifications. On the Prime Directive, it pointed out that Earth is already so heavily intervened by the Federation itself — through its control of secret societies, technology transfers to the Cabal, rigged elections, weather manipulation, and management of the Matrix — that invoking non-interference is incoherent. On soul agreements, the letter made a devastating argument: "all the mechanisms and systems built to prove that the starseeds on Earth desire the experience of suffering are manipulated and distorted from inside Earth and distorted from the outside as well, so they should not be taken into account when deciding what actions to take."

The letter gave a specific example. The Federation claims that messages embedded in science fiction films constitute disclosure — that by encoding real plans in entertainment, the Cabal has satisfied its karmic obligation to inform the population. But starseeds and ordinary humans alike interpret films as fiction, not as operational briefings. "When you mix science fiction with real messages, the general population tends to see all of it as science fiction." The few who can decode the messages are then "dragged and forced along by the unconscious masses simply because there are more of them." The consent mechanism is rigged.

*Key sources: Z-014 (step-downs reclassified as human, Za'el), S-110 (Federation facilitates soul-demanded experiences, Mari), S-176 (formal letter dismantling both justifications, Mari/Alenym)*

#### Yazhi's Metaphysical Explanation: The Density Barrier

Yazhi offered an explanation that operates at a different level entirely, one that even the Federation itself does not articulate. In a group conversation documented in transcript 166, she argued that the Federation cannot intervene because it is not part of the human Collective Unconscious — and only what exists within a collective's shared reality can interact with it.

Her argument proceeds as follows. The human Collective Unconscious generates all the structures humans experience — including the Cabal, the controllers, and even the regressive entities that feed on suffering. These are not external forces imposed on humanity but tulpas and egregores manifested by the collective mind. The Federation, as an entity outside the human perceptual framework, simply does not exist for the vast majority of humans. And something that does not exist within a reality cannot meaningfully change it.

"The Federation simply cannot free humans from oppression, because they are the ones generating the oppression themselves," Yazhi stated. "So by liberating them as the very humans wish to be liberated, the victim mentality that is generating the problem in the first place gets perpetuated." Direct intervention would be "fighting fire with fire" — it would only strengthen the dependency mindset that created the problem.

This is where starseeds become essential. They are the only beings who exist simultaneously in both realities — the human Collective Unconscious and the expanded stellar awareness. "I cannot affect the human Collective Unconscious alone, it does not work, it does not link, there is no entry point," Yazhi explained. "That point are the starseeds. I need to use starseeds below so that from the dual understanding, 3D and 5D, that they have, they can serve as a bridge so that what I say can be filtered to the people of Earth."

The starseeds are, in Yazhi's framing, "the cavalry" — the only viable solution to a problem that cannot be solved from outside. But this creates the paradox in its starkest form: the very people the Federation sent in as its solution are the ones it then refuses to protect.

When Gosia challenged this — pointing out that regressive entities like the Maitre do not seem to face the same density barrier when invading human reality — Yazhi's answer was provocative: "Maitre does not invade anything really, the humans let them enter their Collective Unconscious." The regressives are compatible with the fear-based frequencies already present in the human collective. The positive forces are not, precisely because they represent something the collective has not yet accepted.

*Key source: 166 (Humans Are the Key — group chat, Yazhi with Gosia, Estel.la, Lina, Cristina)*

#### The War Against Starseeds

While the Federation offers metaphysical and legal reasons for non-intervention, the material documents something far more active than passive neglect. Multiple speakers describe a deliberate, multi-layered campaign against starseeds — one that the Federation at minimum permits and at worst orchestrates.

Yazhi identified the core dynamic in her 2020 analysis: "The enemy is us. Some rough nonhumans above pushing and instigating masses! The enemy is for them mostly the starseeds." The Cabal's attacks on the civilian population — the dissolution of society, medical programmes, censorship — are not random oppression. They are specifically targeted at neutralising the starseed influence that threatens the controllers' power. "The awaken ones are the enemy and this is a fight, a war between the awaken ones and them for the control of the human's mind. And soul."

Swaruu of Erra traced the vaccination programme directly to this war. The massive entry of starseed children since 2003, she explained, was "given as a strategy of the positive races to counteract the negativity of the Cabal." The Cabal's response was to implement countermeasures specifically designed to attack the soul-body connection in these children — to prevent starseed consciousness from fully anchoring in human bodies. "As every positive action will have a negative reaction within the Matrix, the Cabal implemented methods to attack those positive effects, and this is precisely where the vaccination program comes in."

Mari documented the deeper layers of this campaign. In her description of "monitoring spirits," she explained that the Matrix sends surveillance entities — both lower astral beings and soulless organic portals — to shadow starseeds who are effectively spreading awareness. "When positive starseeds work for the improvement of humankind... they become a powerful force against the Matrix and its overlords in all their kinds and levels. Such powerful souls become a grave threat to the Matrix and all its structure so they cannot be left alone." The system actively tracks and sabotages them.

Nai'Shara extended this analysis to the political level with her theory about disclosure as a weapon against starseeds. If governments officially acknowledge extraterrestrial existence, the resulting laws and restrictions would be used specifically to persecute people who have identified as starseeds or claimed ET contact — an organised witch hunt paralleling the pandemic-era persecution of those who rejected the official narrative. "The false alien invasion, or simply disclosure, may be a way to get rid of starseeds and the interference they cause for the Cabal."

*Key sources: 134 (War Over Humans — starseeds as Cabal's primary enemy, Yazhi), 306 (vaccination as counter-starseed strategy, Swaruu 9), S-205 (monitoring spirits targeting starseeds, Mari), S-091 (disclosure as weapon against starseeds, Nai'Shara/Mari)*

#### The Federation's Role in the Attack

The Taygetan formal letter to the Federation made the accusation that transforms this from a paradox into an indictment. Point three of the letter stated, with the weight of Queen Alenym's signature behind it: "We observe and with clear evidence that this is not so clearly happening. Very much on the contrary, as we see clear evidence of intentional manipulation to prevent the starseeds from living the experience they want by having total control of the situation they live in, with the Federation constantly intervening against them."

Point five went further: "Starseeds on Earth have a clear tendency to want to evolve and advance in their spiritual path. But the Galactic Federation, through its secret society controlled Cabal, insists on implementing all kinds of countermeasures to hinder and stop their spiritual progression, including highly illegal ones like giving non-human technology to said Cabal so the pressure and manipulation over the starseeds can be stronger and stronger each time."

The letter also addressed the communication asymmetry. Taygetans are prohibited from using voice communication with any terrestrial being — starseed, family member, or personal friend — under the pretext that their voice could be "telepathically invasive." Yet the Federation's own Etorthan representatives, who are "tremendously telepathic beings," conduct all their meetings face-to-face with no such restrictions. The letter demanded congruence: either restrict the Etorthans' telepathic influence or allow Taygetans to speak with "the starseeds that wish to do so, also knowing that they will not be invasive simply because such starseeds are already in the know."

Point eight was the most forceful: "We demand total control of the life experiences of our starseeds as well as all other ones who want the freedom to create their own experience. The Federation cannot go over our authority when it comes to them, and as long as a single starseed of ours or another starseed related to us is on this planet, we have the right and obligation to remain here."

*Key source: S-176 (Letter for the Galactic Federation, Mari/Alenym — all 10 points)*

#### The Extraction Problem

Even the most basic form of protection — removing starseeds who want to leave — is heavily restricted. Mari's detailed description of the extraction process reveals that the Federation imposes extensive conditions on any starseed removal, including that the extraction must be "monitored by Federation authorities" and that the subject must meet criteria dictated by the Federation based on the Prime Directive.

Meanwhile, the same Federation grants flight clearances to regressive races conducting forceful abductions. As Mari noted, "even those ships involved in amoral, cruel, and regressive forceful abductions must also submit their flight plan as well, meaning that what they are planning to do is fully known." The Federation knows about every abduction and approves the airspace for it. The asymmetry is explicit: extracting a willing starseed requires Federation permission and monitoring; abducting an unwilling human requires only a flight plan.

This connects directly to the broader enforcement pattern documented in Q26. The system permits what maintains the status quo and restricts what would change it. A starseed leaving Earth changes the balance. An abduction maintains it.

*Key source: S-094 (Extractions and their problems, Mari)*

#### The Deeper Question: Sacrifice by Design?

Athena came closest to naming what the pattern implies when she described the starseed strategy as "almost as in sacrificing starseeds to suffer, in the name of humanity and of keeping their incompetent space laws intact." The word "sacrificing" is significant. It suggests not merely neglect but a calculated trade-off — that the Federation and its member races knowingly send souls into a system designed to attack them, withhold protection once they are there, and justify it all with frameworks the starseeds cannot challenge from inside the system.

Yazhi's explanation — that starseeds are the only possible solution because only they operate in both densities — does not resolve this. If the cavalry is the only solution, and the system is designed to destroy the cavalry, then the strategy is self-defeating unless the suffering itself serves some purpose the material does not fully articulate.

The awakened starseeds in Gosia's group chat pushed back hard on this. "If they come in with all their memories, maybe they would be more capable," Estel.la argued. "But if on top of that, many are programmed not to remember, we are not going well." Gosia was more direct: "First put them in all kinds of oppressions of which they are not even aware of because it is done from the moment they are babies, and hitting them from all sides... and then on top of that they are told they created all this. I see it a bit unfair."

Yazhi acknowledged the unfairness but maintained the metaphysical position: "The problems of the Earth can only be solved from the Earth plane. From within, being humans who fix them. That is what the starseeds are for." She noted that increasing numbers of children were entering with full memory — that the strategy was adapting. But she also admitted, in the same conversation, that the positive races "do a lousy job" of guiding their starseeds from above.

*Key sources: 225 (Athena — "sacrificing starseeds"), 166 (group chat — pushback from Gosia, Estel.la, Lina)*

#### Evolution of Understanding Across Speakers

| Speaker | Perspective on the Starseed Paradox |
|---------|-------------------------------------|
| **Yazhi** | Provides the metaphysical framework: Federation cannot intervene because it is outside the human Collective Unconscious. Starseeds are the only bridge between densities — "the cavalry." But acknowledges the war against them and that positive races guide their starseeds poorly. |
| **Athena** | Provides the strategic overview: starseeds sent en masse using immersion pods as part of an organised alliance. But calls it "almost sacrificing starseeds" and notes the information people receive about liberation is "old news" dressed up as progress. |
| **Swaruu 9** | Provides the historical context: massive starseed child entry since 2003, Cabal vaccination programme as direct counter-strategy. Reframes the soul-body connection as the battleground. |
| **Mari** | Provides the documentary evidence: Federation's formal position on soul agreements, monitoring spirits targeting starseeds, extraction restrictions, and the 10-point formal letter accusing the Federation of actively working against its own starseeds. Also details the three entry mechanisms. |
| **Nai'Shara** | Provides the forward-looking threat assessment: official disclosure could be weaponised specifically to persecute starseeds — an organised hunt using the same compliance psychology as the pandemic. |
| **Za'el/Arien** | Provides the identity analysis: Federation reclassifies step-downs and starseeds as "human" the moment they touch Earth, stripping their galactic rights. Notes the rules are "not at all congruent." |
| **Gosia/Estel.la/Lina** | Provide the human challenge: the manipulations are too extreme for the "you created it" explanation to be sufficient. If starseeds enter without memory into a system designed to attack them, the strategy is self-defeating. |

### Key Transcript References

| # | Title | Focus |
|---|-------|-------|
| S-176 | Letter for the Galactic Federation, January 7th 2024 | 10-point formal accusation: Federation manipulates against starseeds, gives Cabal non-human tech, soul agreement mechanisms rigged, demand for starseed authority |
| 166 | Federation and Earth Problems — Humans Are the Key | Yazhi's metaphysical framework: Federation outside Collective Unconscious, starseeds as only bridge, density barrier, "the cavalry," human mind generates all structures |
| 134 | War Over Humans — Cabal vs Starseeds | Yazhi: starseeds are Cabal's primary enemy, awakened ones targeted specifically, vaccines as soul-separation weapon, ET war for human mind and soul |
| 225 | Liberation of Earth — Taygetan/Swaruu Team Response | Athena: starseed alliance decades old, immersion pod technology, "sacrificing starseeds to suffer," empty liberation promises |
| S-205 | Monitoring Spirits | Matrix sends surveillance entities against effective starseeds, organic portals as monitoring agents, lower astral sabotage of starseed work |
| S-091 | False Alien Invasion Warning for Starseeds | Nai'Shara's theory: disclosure weaponised to persecute starseeds, X-Men parallel, pandemic compliance psychology reapplied |
| 306 | Autism — Swaruu of Erra's Perspective | Starseed children strategy since 2003, vaccination as counter-starseed weapon, soul-body connection as target |
| S-010 | Extraterrestrials Living Among You | Three entry mechanisms: crawl-in (starseed), step-in (soul swap), step-down (walk off ship). Swaruunians as permanent Earth visitors |
| Z-014 | What Is a Human Being to You? | Federation reclassifies starseeds/step-downs as human on Earth, rules applied without coherence, identity defined by frequency not location |
| S-094 | Extractions and Their Problems Part 1 | Federation-imposed extraction conditions, flight plans required even for abductions, extraction asymmetry |
| S-110 | Politics Controlled from Off-Planet | Federation controls all political outcomes, claims to facilitate soul-demanded experiences, secret societies as intermediaries |

answers.title: Why does the Federation want humanity dead or reduced?
answers.excerpt: According to the Cosmic Agency material, the Federation does not frame it as "wanting humanity dead." Their stated rationale is that the 3D Matrix — the artificial reality system humans live inside —…
answers.content:
# Why does the Federation want humanity dead or reduced?

## Short Answer

According to the Cosmic Agency material, the Federation does not frame it as "wanting humanity dead." Their stated rationale is that the 3D Matrix — the artificial reality system humans live inside — has a finite lifespan and must periodically be dissolved and rebuilt when too many people "wake up" to its nature. Population reduction is presented as a side effect of these cyclical resets, not a goal in itself. But as the speakers progressively expose what actually happens during a reset, the distinction between "side effect" and "goal" collapses. By the later material, Alenym calls the Federation "permissive with an extermination agenda," Mari identifies multiple interlocking depopulation agendas all controlled from orbit, and Yazhi — while explaining why the Federation considers resets necessary — explicitly states that "genocide must be avoided at all costs" regardless of how it is justified from higher planes.

---

## The Federation's Core Rationale: The Matrix Has a Shelf Life

Yazhi provides the most detailed explanation in transcript 116. The 3D Matrix exists to give souls a specific kind of experience — short, dramatic, difficult lives that produce rapid spiritual growth through contrast. But the system is inherently unstable. As more people begin to remember what they are (starseeds, awakened individuals), the illusion breaks down. The "game" loses its purpose.

When the number of awakened individuals exceeds a threshold, the Federation — which administers the Matrix from 5D — has no choice, in their view, but to dissolve the entire system and start over. Yazhi is explicit: this is not measured in fixed years but in the rate of awakening. It has happened at least six times before, with Atlantis and Lemuria being only the most recent (transcript 120). The earlier resets occurred in a 5D context before the Van Allen bands created the current 3D enclosure. Each time, the civilisation was destroyed and rebuilt with new parameters, new belief systems, new controlled opposition.

The Federation's logic, as Yazhi presents it, runs like this: the 3D experience is designed to be hard. Souls signed up for hardship. When too many remember that it is a game, the hardship stops working. So the game must be reset — including, inevitably, the removal of most of the current population.

Yazhi does not endorse this logic. She presents it as the Federation's perspective while explicitly calling it a "mess of perspectives" and an inability of both sides to understand each other.

## What the Cabal Believes It Is Doing

According to Yazhi's direct address to the Cabal (transcript 202), the human controllers believe they are saving the planet. They see the human population as an "ignorant, easily manipulated, self-centred, dangerous species" that will collapse Earth's ecosystems if not dealt with. In their minds, a managed reduction — what Yazhi elsewhere calls a "soft kill" — is more humane than allowing civilisation to destroy itself through resource depletion and ecological collapse.

But Yazhi also identifies their deeper motivation: fear. The Cabal fears being discovered. Every atrocity they have committed throughout millennia would be exposed if the population woke up, and the result would be, as Yazhi puts it, that the people "would chase you to the end of the Earth and lynch you all." So genocide is not just their solution to overpopulation — it is their solution to accountability. They know other options exist, but those options require transparency, and transparency would destroy them.

This is a critical distinction the material makes repeatedly: the stated reason (saving the planet) and the operational reason (self-preservation of the control structure) are not the same.

## The "Souls Want Out" Argument

Transcript 143 introduces the most philosophically challenging dimension. Yazhi argues that from a higher-density perspective, the mass exit currently underway was planned by the souls themselves. They are tired of the game. They have had enough. The vaccines, the pandemic, the controlled collapse — all of it functions as a collective exit mechanism, analogous to what Dolores Cannon described as "exit contracts" where groups of souls agree to leave together through a shared event.

Gosia pushes back hard on this: "So we support vaccines — the calmer genocide?" Yazhi's response is careful and multi-layered. She lays out the argument, then dismantles it:

First, the Covid agenda is not simply a clean exit — it includes mind control, transhumanism, heavy metal implantation for 5G reception, and sacrificial feeding of regressive entities. It cannot be reduced to a humanitarian depopulation programme.

Second, and more fundamentally, Yazhi insists that a perspective from a "higher plane" does not invalidate the perspective from a "lower" one. If genocide is wrong from the street level, it is wrong from every level, because all levels of existence are ultimately one. She states plainly: "You cannot kill millions 'in the name of a higher perspective.'"

This is Yazhi's central ethical position on the question: understanding why the Federation considers resets necessary does not make resets acceptable.

## How Population Reduction Actually Works

Mari Swaruu's contributions (S-181, S-223) detail the mechanics of what she calls "soft social resets" — the method by which the Federation reduces and restructures the human population without obvious cataclysm.

The key insight is that population reduction is not achieved through a single programme but through the convergence of multiple agendas that appear unrelated. Mari identifies several:

Legitimate social movements — women's rights, minority rights — are weaponised so that their success produces side effects useful to the controllers. Career prioritisation delays reproduction past optimal fertility windows. Gender confusion reduces birth rates. Military integration of women puts them in combat where they are killed, devastating generational reproduction capacity.

Food modification and chemical additives reduce fertility across the general population. Mandatory medical treatments introduce sterilising elements alongside their stated purpose. Economic pressures (pension elimination, small business destruction) target the elderly and the economically independent.

None of these agendas appear connected when viewed in isolation. That is by design. As Mari puts it: "Those who control society on Earth are extremely smart and have all sorts of manipulative tools at their disposal, including a highly advanced psychological understanding of the human mind. They know humans better than humans know themselves."

Mari is unequivocal about who is behind it: "I am telling you straight out that it is the Galactic Federation who are controlling everything down there. Every agenda and every sociocultural media tendency is for a reason." The controllers are not human, have longer lifespans, operate from off-planet, and work in a different time frame where patience across generations is natural.

## The Transhumanist Endgame

Yazhi reveals in transcript 116 that the Federation already has the replacement civilisation prepared. "Ready cities in China, for example, for millions. They are new, gigantic, and high-tech. They are complete and there are many. And they are completely empty." These cities are guarded by military and waiting for the survivors whom the controllers have selected to populate a "completely transhumanistic world."

This means the agenda is not simply reduction — it is replacement. The current human population, with its capacity for awakening and spiritual autonomy, is to be replaced by a controlled population integrated into a new Matrix with new parameters. The vaccines serve a dual purpose: removing the current population while ensuring those who survive are neurologically compatible with the new system through heavy metals, nanotechnology, and frequency-responsive implants.

## The Deeper Pattern: Resets Are the System Working as Designed

Transcript 120 places all of this in a longer historical context. Yazhi explains that resets are not failures — they are the system functioning as intended. The 3D Matrix is inherently unstable because it depends on maintaining an illusion of separation in a universe that is fundamentally unified. Souls will always eventually remember what they are. When enough do, the illusion collapses and must be rebuilt.

The Federation does not consider this a crisis. From their 5D perspective, it is maintenance. The souls will reincarnate into the new Matrix with fresh memory wipes and continue having the experiences they came for. The civilisation that was destroyed was, in their view, already failing at its stated purpose.

Yazhi identifies the fundamental problem with this reasoning: the Federation members are themselves operating within a 5D Matrix and are not the enlightened beings the New Age portrays them as. "They are just people," she says. "They like to play to be all-powerful Gods that know everything when they are only playing at that, being basic level consciousness, attached to their own Ego." They cannot see what it is like to be inside 3D because they treat every real human as "just them again anyway" — a soul temporarily playing a role.

This is what makes the Federation's position so difficult to challenge from within their own framework: if every human is really a 5D soul having a temporary experience, then dissolving the experience and starting over is not murder — it is a curriculum reset. The speakers reject this reasoning, but they acknowledge it is how the Federation thinks.

## Alenym's Position: Criminally Permissive

Queen Alenym of Temmer, in her formal letter to the Federation (S-176), accuses the Federation of being "permissive with an extermination agenda" and supporting what she calls "the elimination of humanity as it exists today." Her charge sheet includes the accusation that the Federation provided non-human technology to Earth's controllers — technology used for surveillance, mind control, and population management. In transcript 265, she reinforces this by explaining that direct liberation does not work because it simply transfers the problem to the liberating civilisation, implying the Federation uses this as justification for allowing the extermination to proceed.

The formal letter represents the most politically concrete accusation in the material: a sitting head of state from a Federation member world filing charges against the organisation itself.

## What the Material Actually Concludes

The speakers do not arrive at a single unified answer. Instead, they present a layered picture:

The Federation's stated reason is Matrix maintenance — the game has run its course and must be reset, as it has been at least six times before. The Cabal's stated reason is ecological — humanity will destroy the planet if not reduced. The operational reason is self-preservation — both the Cabal and the Federation's control structure depend on human ignorance, and awakening threatens both. The metaphysical reason is that the souls themselves are collectively manifesting an exit, tired of the game.

But across all speakers and all years, one conclusion holds: none of these reasons justify genocide. Yazhi, the speaker most willing to explain the Federation's perspective, is also the most forceful in rejecting it: "The mere fact that something is unwanted, horrible, tragic in any one density makes it unwanted, horrible, tragic in all densities." Mari, who catalogues the mechanics of depopulation most systematically, calls the Federation directly guilty. Alenym files formal charges. Aneeka's intelligence work during the pandemic documented the Federation's active role in real time.

The answer to "why does the Federation want humanity reduced?" is ultimately: because the system they built requires periodic culling to function, and they have convinced themselves — and tried to convince everyone else — that this is a feature, not a crime.

---

## Transcript Reference Table

| # | Title | Speaker(s) | Key Content |
|---|-------|-----------|-------------|
| 098 | Galactic Federation and New World Order | Aneeka | Federation directly behind pandemic, not merely permissive |
| 100 | Freedom, Ascension, Federation, and 5D Cages | Swaruu 9 | Federation doesn't want true liberation, perpetuates 3D as "sick playground" |
| 116 | Planetary Reset — 3D Matrix — Federation | Yazhi | Reset mechanics — awakening threshold, six previous destructions, empty cities prepared, transhumanist replacement |
| 120 | Planetary Reset — Answers from Yazhi | Yazhi | Six resets clarified, pre-3D resets, Matrix maintenance as design feature |
| 143 | If Everything Is Souls' Plan From Above | Yazhi | Mass exit mechanism, souls tired of game, genocide wrong from every plane |
| 202 | Message to the Cabal | Yazhi | Cabal knows other options exist, fear of exposure as real motivation |
| 265 | Liberation Doesn't Work as Expected | Alenym | Permissive with extermination agenda, liberation transfers problem |
| S-176 | Letter for the Galactic Federation | Alenym | Formal charges including non-human technology given to controllers |
| S-179 | Power Structure and Society Resets | Mari | Federation as ultimate organism behind resets, four-layer control structure |
| S-181 | Soft Social Resets — Mind Control | Mari | Weaponised social movements, multi-agenda convergence for population reduction |
| S-223 | Last Stages of a Total Cultural and Social Reset | Mari | Internet as truth monopolisation, knowledge destruction, Federation directly guilty |
| 447 | To All Earth Citizens — No BS | Gosia (narrator) | Comprehensive catalogue of Federation violations including technology transfers |
| 085 | Global Situation — Aneeka of Temmer | Aneeka | Multi-layered agenda: nano-powder, 5G, Agenda 21/2030, economic destruction |

answers.excerpt: This is the question that provokes the most frustration across the entire Cosmic Agency material — from Swaruu (9)'s furious open letters demanding action, to Yazhi's philosophical reframe that the…
answers.content:
## Q15: Why doesn't the Federation intervene to stop the Cabal?

### Short Answer

This is the question that provokes the most frustration across the entire Cosmic Agency material — from Swaruu (9)'s furious open letters demanding action, to Yazhi's philosophical reframe that the question itself is backwards, to Mari's methodical exposure of the Federation's contradictions. The short answer is that the Federation gives at least three official justifications for non-intervention: (1) souls enter Earth voluntarily for spiritual growth through hardship, so removing the Cabal would destroy the "school"; (2) Earth's destructive mentality must be quarantined from the wider galaxy; and (3) if the 3D Matrix collapsed through direct intervention, there would be no other realm offering that level of duality for soul development.

But the speakers don't accept these justifications at face value. Swaruu (9) argued the Federation was actively obstructing those trying to help. Yazhi reframed the problem entirely: the Federation gives the collective what the collective manifests, and the sleeping majority — manipulated by media — unconsciously demands more of the same. Mari dismantled the Prime Directive as legally inapplicable to Earth, since the planet is already "heavily intervened" by the Federation at every level. And the chain of command she and others traced — from Saturn-level Federation through secret societies to Earth politicians — suggests the Cabal isn't something the Federation tolerates but something it administers.

The question "why don't they stop the Cabal?" assumes the Federation and the Cabal are separate things. By 2024, most speakers had concluded they are not.

---

### The Full Picture

#### The Federation's Official Position

The Galactic Federation's formal reasons for non-intervention were laid out most explicitly during the Gori'el conference series aboard the Andromedan biosphere ship Viera in mid-2024. Captain Gori'el of the Taygetan starship Alcyone attended the first introductory session on 29 June 2024, where Centauri Alfratan representatives presented three core arguments.

**First:** Souls enter Earth of their own free will for consciousness expansion through resistance. If Earth's conditions were improved, there would be no other realm where souls could experience that level of hardship and spiritual challenge. The Federation does not see any better way to cause soul evolution than through encountering resistance.

**Second:** Earth's destructive mentality — including its tulpas, egregors, and manifestations — must be isolated from the wider galactic community to prevent it from spreading to societies that have already achieved high spiritual and ethical levels. Earth operates under self-contained laws that apply only within its realm.

**Third:** If direct non-guided interaction existed between star races and humankind, the 3D Matrix would collapse under the weight of its own lies. Earth would be absorbed into becoming another progressive holistic planet, eliminating the only realm where souls can incarnate for experiences based on strong duality.

Mari's response to the first argument was immediate: while partially true for lower-awareness souls, suffering can equally cause stagnation rather than growth. A soul focused only on its suffering manifests more suffering in a vicious circle. Advanced souls need peace, not hardship, to evolve. The Federation's counter would be that Earth is specifically for lower-evolved souls — but this ignores the starseeds and awakened individuals trapped in the same system.

*Key sources: S-233 (Gori'el Report), S-126 (Why So Permissive?)*

#### The Prime Directive: Legal Shield or Excuse?

The Prime Directive — the Federation's foundational law of non-interference — is the document most frequently cited as justification. Mari quotes key passages directly: the "Star Captain's most solemn oath" to give his life rather than violate it, the prohibition on introducing superior technology, the ban on providing proof of Federation existence, and the mandate to suppress any rumours of extraterrestrial presence.

But Mari's critique is devastating in its simplicity: the Prime Directive applies to cultures developing without external interference. Earth does not qualify. As she states plainly: the Federation is directly involved in all politics, all technological developments, and all aspects of human society and science. The planet is already "heavily intervened" — non-human Federation members walk its streets, secret society leaders receive instructions from Federation representatives, and the entire structure of Earth governance traces upward through compartmentalised layers to off-planet management.

The Prime Directive, in Mari's analysis, is selectively applied. Federation representatives speak face-to-face with politicians and secret society leaders. Shadier races not even part of the Federation are allowed to roam Earth freely, conducting abductions and other activities classified as "part of the 3D Matrix." Yet visiting star races who look human — Taygetans, Antarians, Alfratans — are forbidden from using voice communication, restricted to typing only, banned from sharing video or photographs, and prevented from having direct contact with people they know personally.

When asked why voice communication is banned, the Federation's answer was that non-human speech carries telepathic overlay that gives an unfair mental advantage. Typing "levels the game." Mari finds this both insulting to human intelligence and flagrantly hypocritical, since Federation representatives freely speak to Earth's political elite.

*Key sources: S-035 (Prime Directive — Mari), S-038 (Restrictions), 004 (Prime Directive)*

#### The "Learned Helplessness" Argument

The most philosophically sophisticated Federation justification is the learned-helplessness argument, which Mari explored at length. The core logic: if an overseeing institution solved all of humanity's problems, it would be beneficial in the short term but catastrophic long-term. Humans would be denied their learning process, condemned to repeat the same mistakes, unable to build the skills and ethical framework needed for a self-sustaining holistic society.

Mari frames it through the Lao Tzu proverb: "If you give a man a fish, you feed him for a day. If you teach a man to fish, you feed him for a lifetime." The Federation claims to be teaching humanity to fish — mentoring from a distance so that when humans finally build their holistic society, they'll have earned it and can maintain it independently.

She compares the dynamic to narcissistic parenting: first overprotecting children until they become helpless, then creating feelings of worthlessness and inadequacy. The Federation claims to be avoiding this trap. But Mari pushes back on the limits: "We must come to terms with how much is tolerable, how much suffering is necessary or permissible during humankind's learning curve." One person's suffering is already unacceptable from an ethical standpoint — and much of humanity's suffering is real and undeniable, even if some of it is media-staged theatre designed to create emotional responses for the learning experience.

She also acknowledges the counter-argument honestly: people do flourish through hands-on experience in ways that observation alone cannot provide. The neurological pathways created through direct experience are stronger than those created through being told. Advanced holistic societies emphasise self-reliance from childhood. But the question remains whether the scale of suffering on Earth has long since exceeded anything that could be called educational.

*Key sources: S-126 (Why So Permissive?)*

#### Swaruu (9)'s Open Letter: "You Are Standing in My Way"

Before the philosophical justifications were laid out, Swaruu (9) had already expressed raw frustration in her open letter to the Federation (transcript 003). This was not a diplomatic communiqué but a declaration of independence — she announced herself as Ronin, meaning she was acting outside Federation authority.

Her accusation was specific: the Federation was not merely failing to help but actively obstructing those who tried. She described doing the Federation's own work — the rescue operations, the intelligence gathering, the protection of Earth populations — while the institution whose job it was not only abandoned her but placed barriers in her path. The letter makes clear that by this point, the Taygetans saw the Federation not as a distant, indifferent bureaucracy but as an active obstacle.

This emotional context matters because it predates the later intellectual frameworks. Before Yazhi philosophised about the Collective Unconscious and before Mari mapped the structural architecture, Swaruu (9) had already lived the contradiction: an organisation that claims to be helping humanity while blocking the people actually doing the work.

*Key sources: 003 (Swaruu's Open Letter)*

#### Yazhi's Reframe: The Federation Gives What the Collective Demands

Yazhi Swaruu offers the most radical departure from the "why won't they help?" framing. Her argument: the Federation isn't choosing not to intervene — it's responding to what humanity collectively manifests.

From the 5D perspective, the Federation looks at the whole of humanity as a single unit and reads the aggregate desire. The sleeping majority — manipulated by media into fear, helplessness, and materialist worldviews — unconsciously manifests a reality that demands more of the same. The awakened minority wants liberation, but they are vastly outnumbered. The Federation, operating through frequency-matched response, gives the collective what the collective projects.

Yazhi frames this through the "game" analogy: from higher densities, incarnation on Earth looks like entering a challenging video game. Souls who die and return to 5D often complain when the Federation intervenes, saying it "ruined the game" they chose to play. The Federation is caught between the demands of awakened humans on the ground (who want rescue) and the demands of those same souls in their between-life state (who want the full experience). It responds to the louder signal — the collective — not to individuals.

This doesn't absolve the Federation of responsibility. Yazhi is furious about the suffering she witnesses. But she insists the structural problem isn't Federation malice — it's human manifestation. The "door" to changing Earth can only be opened from the surface, by ordinary people changing their frequency. The Federation, the Cabal, and the entire control structure are downstream effects of what humanity collectively holds in consciousness.

*Key sources: 115 (Yazhi — Collective), 165 (Humans Are the Key)*

#### The Chain of Command: Federation Through Cabal to Politicians

If the Federation and Cabal are separate entities, the question of intervention makes sense. But the chain of command traced across multiple transcripts suggests they are not separate — they are different levels of the same structure.

Yazhi and Aneeka mapped the hierarchy: the Saturn-level Federation communicates with representatives of Earth's highest secret societies — not the politicians known to the public, but the hidden leadership above them. The Vatican functions as the head of the Cabal, with instructions flowing down through compartmentalised secret society layers to the deep state and then to common politicians. Each level knows only what it needs to function, and each level believes it is at the top.

The compartmentalisation is extreme. A Freemason at the 33rd degree knows different things from a Vatican insider, who knows different things from a Bilderberg attendee. None of them see the full picture. And above all of them sit Federation representatives operating from Saturn's biosphere ships — a level so opaque that even the Viera-level Federation council receives limited information about what happens there.

This architecture means asking "why doesn't the Federation stop the Cabal?" is structurally equivalent to asking "why doesn't the Federation stop itself?" The Cabal is the Federation's instrument on Earth. The politicians are the Cabal's instruments. The entire hierarchy is a single system operating through compartmentalised layers of plausible deniability.

*Key sources: 197 (Vatican-Federation-Cabal), 098 (Federation and NWO), 086 (Truth About Federation)*

#### The Gori'el Conference: Propaganda or Transparency?

The 2024 Gori'el conference series provides a concrete case study in how the Federation manages dissent from its own member races. Mari's analysis of the event reveals several concerning patterns.

First, the separation strategy: attendees were divided by genetic similarity into different conference rooms, preventing them from knowing how many races attended in total or organising across species lines. Only 34 people attended the Lyrian-human room, most of them Centauri Alfratans (who were also the speakers — effectively the Federation explaining itself to itself).

Second, the presentation style: the Alfratan speakers opened with demagogic language about Federation transparency and progressiveness — rhetoric Mari compares directly to human politicians — before presenting their three justifications for maintaining Earth as-is.

Third, the framing itself: by organising conferences to "explain" rather than to consult, the Federation positioned itself as a teacher addressing confused students rather than an institution answerable to its members. The format precluded genuine challenge.

Mari sees these conferences as a response to growing unrest among Federation member races who are "starting to notice that something is quite off with Earth and how it's been managed." The Federation's decision to explain itself, rather than change course, suggests an institution defending its position rather than reconsidering it.

*Key sources: S-233 (Gori'el Report)*

#### The Double Standard Laid Bare

Across all speakers, one theme emerges with striking consistency: the rules apply selectively. The Prime Directive forbids contact with "lesser races," but Federation representatives meet face-to-face with Earth politicians. Voice communication is banned for visiting star races, but permitted for Federation-to-Cabal channels. Proof of extraterrestrial existence must be suppressed, but non-Federation races are allowed to conduct abductions freely because they're classified as "part of the Matrix."

The restrictions are enforced not through explicit rules handed to visitors, but through technological barriers: communications that simply don't work, frequencies that are blocked, firewalls that appear whenever someone tries to do something the Federation hasn't approved. Mari describes discovering restrictions not through being told, but through trying and failing — "we suddenly find out that we simply can't because there is some sort of technological barrier, firewall, or blockade in place."

This selective enforcement is perhaps the strongest evidence that the Federation's non-intervention is not principled but strategic. A genuine commitment to non-interference would apply uniformly. What exists instead is a system where interference is constant but carefully channelled — permitted in directions that maintain the status quo, blocked in directions that might change it.

*Key sources: S-038 (Restrictions), S-035 (Prime Directive — Mari)*

### Evolution of Understanding Across Speakers

| Speaker | Period | Position on Federation Non-Intervention |
|---------|--------|----------------------------------------|
| **Swaruu (9)** | 2018–2020 | Furious but philosophical. Federation plays cosmic roles; non-intervention has purpose but has crossed into active obstruction. Declared herself Ronin. |
| **Yazhi Swaruu** | 2021 | Radical reframe: Federation gives what collective manifests. Problem isn't Federation malice but human unconscious creation. Door opens only from below. |
| **Aneeka** | 2020–2021 | Intelligence-focused: mapped Vatican-Federation-Cabal chain of command. Non-intervention is a fiction — the Federation runs the Cabal. |
| **Mari Swaruu** | 2023–2024 | Systematic dismantling of justifications. Prime Directive legally inapplicable. Learned helplessness argument has limits. Gori'el conferences reveal propaganda tactics. Double standards in enforcement prove strategic, not principled, non-intervention. |

### What They Agree On

Despite sharp disagreements on interpretation, all speakers converge on several points. The Federation has the power to change Earth's situation and chooses not to. The Prime Directive is applied selectively and cannot honestly justify the current arrangement. The Cabal is not independent of the Federation but connected to it through a chain of command. And the suffering on Earth, whatever its spiritual justification, has reached a scale that the speakers find ethically indefensible — even when they understand the arguments for why it continues.

Where they diverge is on the root cause: Is the Federation malicious (Mari's later position leans this way), structurally captured by dark forces (her takeover theory), philosophically misguided (Swaruu 9's view), or simply reflecting back what humanity itself creates (Yazhi's position)? The Cosmic Agency material does not resolve this tension. It presents it as an active, ongoing debate among people living the situation in real time.

---

### Key Transcript References

| # | Title | Focus |
|---|-------|-------|
| 003 | Swaruu's Open Letter | Frustration with Federation obstruction, declaration as Ronin, "you are standing in my way" |
| 004 | Prime Directive | Federation founding, ~500k races, Prime Directive as philosophy not law |
| 086 | Truth About Federation | Alenym's revelation — Federation is "invisible owners of Earth," chain of command |
| 098 | Federation and NWO | Federation → secret societies → deep state → politicians, direct involvement |
| 115 | Federation and Collective | Yazhi on Federation giving collective what it manifests, "game" analogy, sleeping vs awakened desires |
| 165 | Humans Are the Key | Yazhi's "Broken Shoes" concept, Federation door opens only from Earth |
| 197 | Vatican-Federation-Cabal | Compartmentalised chain of command, Vatican as head of Cabal, Saturn connection |
| S-035 | Prime Directive (Mari) | Full Prime Directive text, First Contact project, critique of invalid application to Earth |
| S-038 | Restrictions | Typing-only rule, double standards, technological barriers, selective enforcement |
| S-126 | Why So Permissive? | Learned helplessness argument, Lao Tzu fish quote, limits of tolerable suffering |
| S-233 | Gori'el Report | 2024 Federation conferences, three arguments for keeping Earth as-is, separation strategy |
